review: My husband and I both desperately needed slippers and so I searched for amazon-prime-eligible slippers and decided on these for him while they were on a great sale (under $10).  He wears them constantly at home, putting them on immediately when he gets home and leaving them by the door (where his outside shoes are) when he leaves for anywhere.  In other words, when he's home, he wears them.  We have a home that is partially carpeted and partially hard wood and we don't wear "outside shoes" in the house.  He says the slippers are good on both surfaces.  We also have stairs and he says they are good on the stairs (doesn't fall out of them as with some slippers).  I only wish I had gotten them for myself as well because the Dearfoams DF118 I got for me that were twice the price and have already become awful (the inside padding moves and becomes very uncomfortable).  I like these Tamaracs so much, I'd like to find them in another color in my size to buy for me.  No more silly pretty slippers for me!  I want comfort and usability.  These slippers have that in spades.  Might not even wait for them to be on sale.  They are much better value than Dearfoams for sure!

review: I searched high and low for the perfect black boot. I wanted to invest in a pair I'd wear for years, instead of throwing money away at cheap boots every season. So glad I chose these.I have short legs and athletic calves, so finding a nice pair of boots is a little hard. I wanted to be able to wear them to work or casual, jeans or leggings tucked in. My calves are about 15" around, and most wide-calf boots I saw in my search looked ridiculous. I tried a pair of wide-calf earth boots and I looked like a swashbuckler.These don't look very wide at the calf, but they have an expanding panel right next to the zipper that is perfectly designed. It stays snug around your calf, but expands to fit jeans while remaining snug so you don't look like a pirate. (If you want to look like a pirate, these boots might not fit the bill, sorry...) The top of the boots hit about an inch or two below my knees.The leather quality is excellent, and I can vouch for the waterproof claim. We have about 8" of snow on the ground right now, and I trudged through the snow with no moisture seeping in. The traction on the bottom of the boots was great, but not so deep that they look like snow boots. Note: these aren't snow boots, they don't have any extra lining for warmth, but they were perfect for the walk from my car to work in the snow. My feet stayed dry and I never slid around.The inner sole of the boot is very comfortable, I expected a break-in period, but never experienced any discomfort. I noticed a few people complain about the zipper in the reviews, but it doesn't bother me. It is silver, and a little closer to the front of the boot, but I thought it was barely noticeable.I couldn't be more pleased with these boots, so happy with this purchase.

review: I started biking for fitness 2 months ago. I started with standard cotton shorts and t-shirts. Once I was able to ride more than 10-15 miles, the cotton garments started showing their limitations. I was a little self-conscious about riding around in standard skin-tight riding shorts so I ended up purchasingPearl Izumi Men's Canyon Shortwhich came with one Pearl Izumi liner. The Canyon Shorts are, how should I say, more socially appropriate to me when I stop off in a gas station to grab some orange juice, or at a diner for lunch.I ended up buying 4 additional liners (Pearl Izumi Men's Liner Short) as I bike 4-5 days per week. I put a liner on under the shorts and go for my ride. The next day I use a new liner with the same pair of Canyon shorts. Once a week I wash all of the liners and the shorts and I am ready for the next week. The liners are much cheaper than comparable stand-alone riding shorts so I saved a lot of money going this way.The liners stay firmly in place while I am riding. The Canyon Short rides up my leg slightly over time but it's not a problem as a quick tug, or standing up on the pedals, every hour or so takes care of the problem.The chamois is comfortable and seems durable so far. I have used some of the liners 5-6 times already and there is no pilling, tearing or other obvious degradation of the material. That being said, it does not feel like you are wearing a diaper, so walking around is not at all uncomfortable.The liner and shorts are great at wicking moisture. Last week a did a 3 hour ride on a 98 degree day with high humidity. When I was done my skin was almost completely dry and the shorts barely felt damp. Perhaps this is the biggest difference I notice with using the appropriate clothing for riding - moisture management makes a big difference over time.There is a small zipper pocket on the right leg of these shorts but I never use it while riding.I highly recommend these shorts and liners to anyone looking for a high-quality, inexpensive, option for riding shorts.
summary: Excellent when paired with the Pearl Izumi liners

review: Stuhrling Original Men's 107EM.331531 Classic Delphi Omega Stainless Steel Automatic Skeleton Watch SetThis is one beautiful watch. As a child I had a hand me down skeleton watch from my dad. It was full glass on the front and back, the front having a moving twirl design with the seconds. The rear was fully visible. It was a memory from my childhood and now is rekindled with this watch. It is a thrill to wear, on a personal level. Getting my personal feelings out of the way, it's still a very attractive watch.With the watch you get two "easy" change watch bands. One is black leather the other is brown. Both are very nice and have a unique (to me) finger nail accessible nub that lets you release the pin. This makes changing the band easy. Well, for most. Me, it's easy to take off but I have trouble aligning it to put it back. Then, that could be just me. I do eventually get it back on but I wish there could be an easier targeting way. I could just be a klutz.The size of the watch is large but not gigantic like my Invicta Russian Diver. It will catch attention and draw compliments. If you mention skeleton, you will be asked to show it a lot. The face is very monochromatic, so the numbers and hands don't pop, but there is elegance in the look so it's just what it is. It glows nicely at night but isn't very bright.  The weight of the watch is hefty but not a problem. It just feels right overall. It is an automatic watch so be prepared to wind it or shake it if you don't wear it every day. You can also get a winder but that will mostly cost half the price of the watch.The word Swiss appears every so often in the literature or descriptions, but nowhere does it say Swiss Made or Made in Switzerland. So, my guess is China. I wish more watch companies claiming Swiss origins actually put a Made in... on the watch somewhere. That's the only strike I can muster and it's not a big one given the price point of this watch. In summary, this is a cool beautiful watch. Wear it in good health!

review: Selecting riding boots can be tough: with so much shoe, it's difficult to find one that fits sleekly but allows room to tuck in leggings/jeans, doesn't gape at the ankle, and, not least, actually feels comfortable to walk in.  There's a LOT of options out there because the equestrian trend is huge right now...After pouring over a lot of reviews, I determined the look and rumored fit of the Jodhpur was a safe bet. Amazon's price was hard-to-beat and, afterall, Cole Haan makes beautiful and dependable footwear. That's why I was so bummed when they arrived.Firstly, I'd never have guessed these were Cole Haans. I ordered in the rich-looking black suede... but in person? Wow, cheap cheap! I'm a bit of a suede snob but I know you don't have to shell out big bucks to experience buttery, thick, supple suede. The quality is not what CH usually churns out--the suede was thin, looked worn-in already (the slightly 'distressed' look is so tiresome) and the side goring was what I'd expect to see on a shoe half this price. I noticed in the few moments I had one on, they also picked up everything. Funny, but my other quality suede simply does not do that. Had these been the 'winter-proof' kind of boots like a La Canadienne or similar rugged brand I may have forgiven the look/feel of the suede. But these are hardly weather-proof...the included CH standard 'don't knick or scuff them' card reminded me these are just for lookin' pretty. So why inferior materials?I ordered in 9 as I'm between 8.5 and 9 and found they were TTS. However, I was so disappointed I didn't even try on the mate. But on the one foot, I could easily tell they would be decently comfy and the width was a steady 'M'.  No surprises.What about the all important fit in the pesky calf area? Too tight, you look like a sausage roll; too loose and it looks like you're trying to smuggle candy bars due to all the gaping.  The side panel goring allows for some stretch and I noted that that the Jodhpur has been cited as athletic/wide calf-friendly. At 14 1/2 inches, my calves are fairly standard and the fit was SNUG. Yes, they'd stretch more, but personally, I'd not consider these if you're over 15 1/5 and you plan to tuck in.Since there is NO ZIPPER, beware if you have a high instep. I don't, so these slipped on with just a bit of wrangling. But having a narrow heel and very low instep may be a requirement if you don't feel like a wrestling match.Most problematic, besides the inferior materials, was the bunching. No, not at the ankle where riding boots tend to slack, wrinkle, and bunch, but higher on the shin. Very weird. These made me appear to have very thin ankles (a good thing) and then a few inches up the suede stuck out quite awkwardly, then tightened again at the calf. Not a flattering silhouette. Very weird. It's like I had square blocks attached to the front of my shins.Even if you think the Jodhpur may work, fit wise, if you're a fan of Cole Haan, you'll know this particular pair is not up to their usual standards. These went back the following day. At this price point, you can do much, much better. So my quest continues.

review: I've actually wanted a pair of Wallabee boots for decades, but just not enough to pony up the money.  In addition to the high price, I also don't like the feel of tall heels, so I wasn't sure that I like the heel on the Wallabees (this is more about comfort then cosmetic.)  But when I found these Gunn's on sale, with their slim soles, I had to grab them and I'm really glad I did.  They look great and they're very comfortable.  Like with many Clarks shoes, the amount of cushioning provided by such a slim sole is amazing.I normally wear a 10.5-11 in shoes and a 10.5 with Clarks.  So, I purchased them in both of those sizes, but have yet to receive the 10.5's, so this review is written on the size 11's, which I think I'll keep.  Because of the square toe, I could probably fit into a 10.5, but I think they would be a little too tight.  I have a little extra room with the 11's, but not anymore then I'd want and I think the size 11 fits perfect on me.  Plus, I'll probably wear them with thick socks some times and there won't be any extra room then.As others have noted, they are a little narrow, but mocs usually do run narrow.  Plus, my feet run a little narrow and this just helps them fit snug on me, regardless of whether I have room to spare in the toes or not.  They do not run particularly long, so don't worry about that.  My suggestion would be to stick with your most common size, like I did, since I usually wear a size 11 in most shoes.Several reviewers mentioned them rubbing around the heel or the shoe opening, and I can understand that.  But while I haven't worn mine enough to say for sure that this won't be a problem for me, I'm not finding any discomfort yet.  That's not usually a problem for me and I don't expect it to be one this time, either, but because they're narrow and have a somewhat hard band around the opening, I can see why some may complain about it.Overall, they look and fit great and they're very comfortable, so I think I'm going to love them!P.S. I purchased the olive pair, and as you may suspect, they're more green then in the pictures here or on the Clarks website, but they're still more of a grayish/brownish green color, which I think looks much better then in the pictures on either site.Update: I received the smaller pair today (e.g. 10.5) and had to try them on to make sure I'd chosen the best size.  Surprisingly, they fit quite well and had more toe room then I'd expected.  Anatomically, the were probably a better fit, because the arch support was more appropriately located (note that I have size 10.5 feet, but fallen arches and long toes often require an 11 shoe size.)  My biggest concern was probably with the width, because they were far more snug, and despite having plenty of room in the toe box, I struggled just to get them on my feet.  In the end, however, I stuck with the size 11 pair to be safe and for a more comfortable fit while wearing with heavy socks.  Either size would have worked, but while I hate for my shoes to be too large, I just thought the smaller size was just a little too snug on me (although, if it were a Summer shoe, I would have stuck with the smaller size.)  So basically, if your feet are big for their shoe size, I'd definitely recommend going with the larger size, but if you have a narrow foot and you're not worried about them fitting snug, you might prefer the smaller.  They are definitely somewhat narrow, but the length is okay.Update 2: Another important point about this shoe is that the shoe opening is very small.  It has an attached tongue, which probably contributes to this.  Part of the reason why I went with the larger size is because I could barely get the smaller size on my foot.  Once it was on, it didn't seem terribly small, but I suspect the snug fit of the shoe opening has something to do with the complaints of rubbing around the heel area.  Putting on the larger size isn't a breeze, either, but it was much easier for me then the smaller one.

review: 6-21-12...I've washed these socks 3 times and they've shrunk each time.  There are no washing instructions on the package so I washed them the way I've always washed white socks....in warm water and warm dryer (not hot).  Won't buy these again and will attempt to return them.  I'll let you know if that's a problem.  If you do buy these be sure to was in cold water and line dry!!!These are decent socks but I do have a few issues with them.  I should note that I'm a woman.  I often buy men's sport socks and sometimes men's sneakers because women's sneakers are usually uncomfortable on my wide feet.  I'm not a big woman but I do have big feet...lol. I just don't like women's socks, especially around the toes. I hate those sloppy sewn seams at the top that are "nobby" on each side.Although these Nike socks are made well they're a little tight at the top on the calf.  The circumference of my calf where the top of the sock comes is 13 inches.  Wouldn't mind just a bit more stretch there.  I purchased size large socks to allow for a wee bit of shrinkage.  Just for reference, I buy anywhere from a size 8 to a 9 regular men's sneaker depending on cut.  There's also a kind of 1 1/2" elasticized band in the material that goes around the instep of the sock which I think will shrink a bit after a few washings.  Don't care for that sqeezing my foot.  I'm sure these will service me for the time being...just not sure I'll buy them again.  By the way..under a sticker they slapped on the label, the retail price is listed as $14.00.  I guess a few extra dollars isn't too high a price to pay for the convenience of buying on line unless you shop in department stores anyway.

review: It's surprising that there really is a difference between the various well-known brands of men's briefs.  I've been looking around to replace my aging drawer-full, and wrote a review of my recent experience with Hanes (in brief, they're inexpensive and show it).I've never liked Jockeys, for one reason:  they advertise their "Exclusive Y-front fly design," and I've consistently had difficulty getting Mr. Johnson through the fly and into use.  Sure, we can giggle about it, but it's no fun having to stand there, reach through the fly, grab Ol' Willie, and pull him through an awkwardly-designed fly every time you've got business to do.  It's more of a hassle than it might sound, and underwear shouldn't be something you have to deal with. And putting the trouser snake back in is even more difficult--it takes two hands!Let me be clear here:  The fly opening is REALLY tight, and no matter what you do, it's REALLY difficult to go through it. You may say, "But they must sell a million of them every year, year after year.  Everybody's heard of Jockey shorts.  This guy must be exaggerating."  Yes, yes, I know.  And you'll see that what I say is true.  You have been warned.I just bought some more to find out whether they still make them this way, and they do. I have no idea why Jockey not only makes them this way, but brags about it.  I guess there are some guys that worry that the fly is too loose, and if you're one of them, you don't have to worry about it with Jockeys--everything is safe and secure--too secure for my liking. They're reasonably priced, and otherwise well made--definitely better constructed than Hanes, for example. But I won't buy them again--too much trouble.

review: Firstly, this watch looks better in the photo than it does in real life. It has a cheap plastic look and a flimsy feel and the band should be ashamed of itself. Now that I have the negatives out of the way let me roll in the positives. You get a good watch for the money with tons of features. Despite what other reviewers have said about bits and pieces breaking and falling off, I have not experienced this. My watch has held up very good.I must admit that I only wear it once a week and have not subjected it to it's true purpose...outdoor activities.As an avid collector and lover of wrist watches I rotate my stock.Features: The alarm is quite loud, louder than my other expeditions, it actually wakes me up. The three times zones are useful for travelling. 24 hour countdown timer is very handy for those who need it, and although I don't use the rotating bezel, others may find this useful as a second timer, for whatever reason. All digital Expeditions have a stopwatch so nothing new there. Indiglo night light is adaquate, with the night mode feature being a bonus.I have found this watch to keep very good time, only losing a few beats per month, like all Timex quartz watches.I recomend this as a good knock around watch, but if you're looking for something really sturdy that will hold up well out in the field then look elsewhere . Go for G Shock or a Pathfinder or a Citizen Protek or the like.All in all a good watch for the hard earned you will outlay.

review: OK, if you want a general overview of this watch, please read my review of the black dial model my son bought.  Now I'm going to get a little technical.  I don't repair watches anymore, but I still have most of my equipment.  Checking the timekeeping of my watch with a 'Vibrograph', I found a 10 sec gain per 24 hrs in dial up, a -1 in pendant right, and a -1 in pendant down.  Since those are the most common positions for a watch in daily wear, I didn't check further.  This averages out to about a 5 second a day gain, or a gain of 1 minute in 12 days.  If that's not good enough for you, got to Wallyworld and buy a cheap quartz watch.  However, for those that appreciate a mechanical timepiece, this is GREAT for a watch that cost me $57.  The SS case will outlast the movement by years, and if this model is still available when this one needs repair, I'll just buy another one.  The watch looks elegant, very classic styling.  If you're not needing a watch that you can use to navigate your starship, get a real watch with real gears.  Enjoy the renaissance quality of a real timepiece.PS: an auto-wind watch needs to be fitted closely to your wrist so the rotor (winding weight) can take full mechanical advantage of your movement to keep the mainspring wound up.  If you want to wear it loosely like a bracelet, it will NEVER stay wound up.Oh, yes, I checked my son's watch too.  His keeps better time than mine but I won't bore you with the details of gravitational error as I did with mine.  I really don't think you can go wrong with this watch as long as you're not on a schedule that requires quartz watch accuracy of seconds per year.  Personally, I don't want to live in that fast lane.

Cotton is comfortable and they are plenty large for layering if you order your normal size . The price is darn near half of other brands at the current sale price . I have learned to not wait and just buy these when I can get a discount at all . These compare to Penny's St Johns Bay shirts , they are higher quality and cut large . They are truly for guys that are big not just tall like the Carhartt's seem to run in each size . I refuse to buy any shirts without a the extra tuck in fabric . These will never show your plumbers crack . Great deal now , the long sleeves even came with hangers I kid you not . For $11 with winter coming these are nice inside the house lounging around in a pair of gym pants . They also are a great layer over a thermal and under a sweater building layers and being large make for allot of trapped air keeping in warmth . Even though it is fall I picked up some short sleeves too for spring to replace the ones I ruined this year . The time to get these is when their discounted . At this price I wouldn't go whole hog but it is rare you find them in the XLT sizes discounted much more as the economy is improving .Colors are flat but nice .

review: These are very nice, comfortable house slippers in their manufacturing concept.  I do like the slightly raised (firm) sole with the soft padding in the footbed and heel.  These two features give much better comfort and support to the foot, arch, and heel, thus keeping a lot of unneeded pressure off the knees and back when walking and standing--much more than other department store, flatbed or foam-cushioned, house slippers could ever offer. It is also nice to have available exact shoe sizes and the adjustable velcro top strap.  If these slippers were only about these features and at a better price, they definitely would deserve 5 stars!Price, though, brings these slippers down to 4 stars.  While these are better slippers than most house/lounge shoes, they do cost considerably more, and I don't think there is that much more comfort, style, or design that warrants the twice or triple price from other shoes available.The next star demerit is due to, since I'm not a sock person, especially in loungewear and walking around the house and never in warm weather months, I find the 2+ inch long embroidered name tag firmly sewn into the fabric of the footbed of both slippers VERY annoying against bare feet.  It would do Daniel Green much better to use a simple iconic logo in much smaller embroidery and without the irritating nylon tag.  Better yet, either on top or on the side of wrap-top of the slipper.  These aren't fashion statements anyway, so if the name has to be there, why not somewhere it is not so annoying to the actual comfort of the feet these shoes are suppose to provide. Socks do alleviate this annoyance, so if you're one that must wear, like to wear socks these shoes wouldn't be a problem for you in that area.  These do work nice on chilly/cold nights when you want to wear thicker socks as the strap does adjust well.
summary: Cushy comfort, well-made, but couple drawbacks

review: I have these as well as the 3 fringe boots and while I love them both, I tend to wear these more. I think the 3 fringe boots are perfect for spring (adorable with dresses and skirt and shorts) while these are better for fall (I have the pile lined ones for winter). I like that I can wear these with straight leg and skinnies and they still look adorable. It looks very casual chic to wear these with ankle length straight leg jeans all frumpled around the top. these are really comfortable and work well as everywhere shoes. I'm considering buying a pair or two in another color to supplement these. I really wear them everywhere and with everything. I can even get away with wearing them to work sometimes. My boss has a pair of the tramper boots as well.They are insanely comfortable, with a nicely padded footbed, soft suede upper, and very flexible sole. The grip on these is not quite as good as the kilty mocs, with the little rubber nubby things, but they are still grippy. I navigated across a sheet of ice yesterday without issue. I have walked up to 4 miles at a time in these without a single blister. While there is very little arch support, I never find that to be painful in Minnetonkas, not sure why. I found them true to size. I have narrow-ish feet with very tapered toes (big toe is the longest). If you have wider feet you might want to size up.These are probably my favorite Minnetonkas I've bought and way way way more versatile than they look.

review: Reading other reviews, I think I expected something different from what other people knew they were getting. But rereading the description, I think I'm justified in finding it inaccurate.I have these pants from Express that I love. They're jeggings, in that they are totally comfy and stretchy, but they don't look a thing like leggings. They just look like jeans. That's what I wanted out of these pants too.Well, heads up: These pants do not look like jeans. They look like leggings. The fabric feels and stretches like leggings. The stitching screams leggings. The button at the top looks like the cheesy plastic buttons on children's clothes. There is nothing jean-like about them. Fake pockets and zip flies are not unique to jeans, and therefore, in my opinion, do not give you license to say that something is "like jeans." For something to be like jeans, it needs to be able to imitate the defining factor in jeans, which is *denim.* If you say "like jeans" and don't mean "like denim" you're deliberately misleading people. These are not jeggings. These are leggings. Let's call a spade a spade.I was so appalled at the cheap look of these pants that I didn't even try them on. However, I did notice that they appear to be very low rise as well. I don't tend to find pants that sit on or below my hip bones comfortable to wear, in the sense that they are very restricting in the crotch area and I'm constantly hiking them up. Such a low rise in pants with this kind of fabric makes me think that they put the wearer at serious risk for camel-toe.Given that I am a self-respecting human being who does not wear leggings out of the house pretending they're pants, I could not keep these. I had to send them back.

review: Don't really expect much from a belt. No one sees them and they're really just to keep my pants covering all the goodies. This belt has changed my perspective. The manufacturer has engineered a masterpiece. The leather comes in a customizable length, simply cut it to fit. On the black leather, the waist lengths are marked, but should be cut a quarter of an inch more as you have to account for the portion that the teeth must pinch down so that the buckle holds in place. On the white leather, the lengths are not marked (or maybe they are, but marked in white which defeats the purpose). The stitching and the inset teeth for the buckle to hold are both well produced and very sturdy. The buckle is beautiful and the release latch is simple. You can't tell it's the latch as it fits flush with the rest of the buckle. The only thing i fear is scratching the buckle. The belt is nice enough for a shirt and tie, possibly suit depending on your taste, and definitely everyday wear. It's a dynamic belt and couldn't recommend it more.
summary: A new staple in the wardrobe

review: I've reviewed at least one other Carhartt shirt and told how much I love these shirts in it. I'll tell you what makes a Carhartt shirt fit some people different. These shirts are wide through the shoulders. It's a basic Physics lesson. If the shoulders on your shirts are tight, the length is going to be pulled up some. These shirts, if ordered in your normal size, will fit you a little bigger than what you're used to. If you have wide shoulders, I'm really not for sure what the average person measures through the shoulders, but you know if you have wide shoulders. I do think the shoulders are the biggest difference in these shirts and other brands.I've washed mine probably 2 times a week for 6 months and can say it still fits like a new one. I've worked in deserts in it, hauled lumber across my shoulder with it on, lifted concrete bags, shingles and it doesn't have a single hole in it. The other thing that is unsettling is that I have to press the buttons really hard in order to get results. It gives me a feeling that the product is inferior or cheap. I am not going to send the watch back because of the hassle of shipping and the cost of it.  I am keeping it because with all its fault, it is a fine looking watch which coming out of the box fooled me.  Thanks.

review: I used this as my wallet during a 2 week trip in Europe.  The Pro's were that it was a very good organizer, and you can pack a lot of stuff in it.  It is Rfid, I'm not sure how important that is in Europe, but it was a nice feature.  The hook and loop worked well as did the zippers.  The nylon material was sturdy.The downside, while walking around with it around my neck under my shirt one day, the neck strap had pulled apart from the wallet at the seam.  Luckily my under shirt was tucked in so I didn't lose it but didn't know it had fell until I went to pay for something.  If I was wearing a tshirt or didn't have a shirt tucked in, it would have slipped right out onto the ground and I would have lost my passport, license, cash, train tickets and credit card.  For the rest of the trip I was forced to put this in my pants pocket where it was vulnerable to pickpockets and I wished I had just brought my regular wallet with me.  I do not recommend buying this product.

review: My husband works near 700 degree ovens that melt glass, so he has to wear ALL cotton thermals to protect him from the heat.  I figured we should try these since theCarhartt Men's Heavyweight Cotton Thermal Crew Neck T-Shirt's are more expensive.  These are heavy duty, they wash well, and they are softer than the Carhartt's.  I don't know if that means they won't last as long, but we will see.I imagine these would keep the body warmer than the Carhartt's since they are softer so the fibers will stay closer to the skin.  That's awesome for their intended purpose, but probably not for my husband since he needs his body heat to escape.These seem like a good quality and would keep a person very warm.  I have to wash them every other night, and they've held up well thus far. review: This bag has the longer straps that will allow you to carry the bag on your shoulders, which is the main reason why I purchased it.  The fabric exterior is very nice.  I do wish it had little metal feet on the bottom to help protect it, but sadly it doesn't. The straps, exterior corner covers, the bottom and the interior top inside had a fake leather or pleather material which I  wasn't in love with, but it wipes off well enough.  Inside I was hoping for a large open space so that I could use my purse insert.  Unfortunately, it has a center zippered pocket which I am not a fan of. Wish I'd seen that in the product description.  I considered cutting this out since the lining is only a thin fabric, but decided against it.  I did keep it anyway because I liked the size which is just right and it fits nicely under your arm and lays close to the body.  It also has a magnetic closure at the top which I really prefer over a zipper. The back of the bag is flat so it won't cause pilling on your clothes like zippers or detailing there often does. Inside it has the regular pockets (two small in front and one larger zippered one in back, along with the center zippered one.  This bag has just enough good features to make it acceptable for a season.Note added after several weeks of use:  The bag ended up being heavier than any bag I have ever owned. I actually noticed that my shoulder would ache after carrying it shopping for an hour.  I had to continually change shoulders.  My husband cut the fake lock off the front for me as it added additional unnecessary weight to the bag.  If you're used to a light bag, this is not the bag for you.  This weight factor has now put this bag into the NOT GOOD ENOUGH category for me.

review: When I was trying to decide which doctor's kit to buy, I was torn between this one and the Fisher Price medical kit (Fisher-Price Medical Kit.) In the end, I actually bought both, and this is why:The Melissa & Doug kit has the adorable jacket and is a good price for all it contains, but it does not have a blood pressure cuff, which is something my son was really interested in. I was also worried about the stethoscope after reading the other reviews. After both kits arrived, I was very glad I had both!The Melissa & Doug stethoscope is, indeed, not very usable. It needed a very specific battery, which I found in the jewelry department of our local major retail store that starts with a "W" and ends with "Mart." Then, after putting the battery in, the stethoscope makes this weird, loud coughing sound when you push the button. It's kind of disturbing, actually. My kids (ages 3 and 1), just stared at it for a while, and then my older child said, "Oh, it's sick!" The stethoscope also doesn't stay in my son's ears, so he just wears it around his neck.There is no storage for all the pieces, either, other than sticking them in all the pockets of the coat. They fall out easily if the doctor's coat is moved, so we ended that pretty quickly. Since I had the Fisher Price kit, we just combined all the medical equipment into that storage bag, and it fit nicely.The thermometer that comes with the M&D; kit is also strange and doesn't resemble any thermometer my kids are used to seeing. I'm very glad I have both kits so the kids can have the best of both worlds! It also makes it easier to have two syringes, since that seems to be the favorite tool, and both kids can have one!Admittedly, the surgical mask is adorable when I put it on the kids, but they won't wear it for more than a few seconds. Oh, and my son wears a size 5T shirt, and the doctor's coat is a little baggy on him, which I think is great since he can use the coat for even longer!

review: I received this watch a couple of days ago. My first impression was good. The packaging was adequate and it arrived when expected. Reading some of the reviews I expected a watch that was bulky and that I would have trouble in adjusting the bracelet to fit me. The size of the watch is just right, not a humungus wrist weight. The watch was light enough and I found wearing it all the time comfortable. The band was easy for me to adjust. I had the small size screwdriver needed and within ten to fifteen minutes, working carefully, I had adjusted it to fit. The atomic time keeping works, in fact it seemed to have worked while in transit. I went on line and got the instructions so by the time I had the watch, I could set up the watch how I wanted, not that much setting up was required. I will definitely have a use for the time record mode. I travel by road a lot so, departure and arrival times are useful, and I can have it accurately recorded by the watch, no hassle. More anon when I have it for a longer time.Just an update as at 9 June 2011. This watch has lived up to expectations. Since I have had it, I have had no issues; the size is fine, I have adjusted the band several times to get the perfect fit, without difficulty. The atomic time keeping is more than impressive, you would be surprised at the coverage of the signal and it still updates without fail. If you go to the WWVB NIST site they give you an idea of the coverage at particular times of the day and night. I should have bought this watch a long time ago.

review: I was looking to replace a pair of 5-year old Solomon Techamphibians, size 10, which are a bit snug on me. So based on the reviews here, I ordered size 10.5 Teva's and was really looking forward to making them my go-to summer shoes this year. Well, they just arrived, and I slipped them on. Within 5 minutes, my large toe started aching. It was pushed right up to the toe-edge of the shoe. I slipped them off and compared it to the same-size Ecco's I purchased a few weeks ago. The Teva's were smaller in length AND width. Also, I noticed there was no arch support. I even slipped on my Solomon's to compare, and they felt better!I am disappointed in the quality of these shoes too. The fact that there is no tongue, means the shoe fits snugly around the foot, and you don't have the option to loosen and open it up, even when you loosen the bungee cord. The inside around the heel and under the tongue area has a stretch nylon fabric that feels cheap. The heel collapses, but is not hinged like the Techamphibians, and is not comfortable. The fine mesh on the interior of the shoe should keep fine sand out of the shoe (assuming it doesn't enter around your ankle).These shoes were a good price on Amazon, but now trying them, has cost me $6.00 for shipping. This is the second bad shoe experience I've had on Amazon. Next time, I'm just heading to the brick and mortar. Gotta try 'em before you buy 'em.

review: EDIT, Wash report:  I machine-washed the 'desert gold' (which is really medium khaki) on warm, delicate, with a load of usually-dry-clean cottons.  Then put them all in the dryer at 'medium cotton' setting, using the moisture sensor for when drying stops (Kenmore).  No shrinkage, and the shawl came out just fine;  no fading, no color running on the other (similar-colored, but lighter) clothing.  Better, in fact, than before I washed it.  Soft.  Still not fond of that color, but it matches my Pendelton khaki slacks (see my review here in Amazon, near same date, on the slacks).  100% Viscose.Original review, follows below.=======Since these are factory seconds, you do gamble that they will be good enough to wear.  At about $3 each (price is higher, now) -- I took a chance on the yellow and the desert gold:  their picture colors are accurate, though the actual scarves don't have much sheen (thank God).  The package came from India, replete with customs declaration on the bag.These are nice scarves, sized as you'd expect for Pashima design.  Happily, there are no annoying tags.  The desert gold (= wet beach sand, a warm khaki) has a run in it; but the yellow one was fine.  I don't have a problem with the former scarf having a run in it.Here, the idea is to have a substitute cover, versus a jacket.  Either scarf-wrap is good enough to wear with nice clothing.  I imagine one could machine wash on delicate or soak cycle, else hand wash in cold water and shampoo or Woolite;  but the removable tape label says 'dry clean only'.  I bet the color will run.  But I also bet you could put this in the dryer, at lowest or air-only setting.  Will try doing that, and will report back here.  At this price, it's worth the risk to test.  Alternately, hand swish-wash with mild shampoo (well-diluted), then drape over the previously-cleaned shower rod (i.e., if you're travelling and need to wash the scarf).What surprises me the most, is that the fabric isn't threadbare.  It's sheer, but nice.  Two or three threads woven together, not merely one-to-one.  Think of silky tights, and you'll have a sense of the feel.  They are like thin t-shirt cotton, in weight.I'd iron them on low heat with sizing, if you want a dressed-up look as a shawl.  If you've no time to iron, just spray the scarf with water until it's damp, and drape over a cleaned shower rod.  They should steam well, but I'd iron them with a thin towel over the fabric, just in case; for viscose means rayon, and it can melt.Tassles are about 5" long, not the 2" in the product description.  I don't like tassles, but they are pretty.If you drape the scarf over your neck, or bunch it up to wear as a scarf, it looks nice.  Could wear as a hood, too, in case of wind.  Since it's Viscose, not silk, I'd not worry about rain.Fabric has a very nice feel, to my mind better than silk (which is sometimes scratchy, itchy or 'hot').
summary: Nice feel, drape, size: worth more than priced

The problem is the fabric is cut with very little seam allowance (to save fabric/money). It is a material made with tiny strands of elastic running through it to make it stretchy. The elastic pulls away from the cut edge and unravels past the seams, making little threads of rubberband-colored elastic stick out all along every seam.On the black gloves, this shows as a frayed, lighter colored bunch of filaments all along the length of the seam up the arms and around every finger. This ruins the effect of a formal look. It would look just as bad on the red or white fabric, I am sure, cause it's rubberband beige frayed "threads". They need to cut bugger seams and lapp the seams to prevent pulling, or heat-seal the cuts or use a different fabric.Like I said, if you need a one night costume or are going to be in a high school play, these will probably be fine. review: I sit by a window at work, and my hands get so cold when typing. I have some thick, heavy knitted fingerless gloves made from thick yarn that keep my hands warm, but they are super bulky, tight around the second knuckle of my fingers making typing hard, and they leave fuzz everywhere. I ordered these gloves because I was looking for something lightweight and stretchy.First, I ordered the coco brown. What I got is not brown, not even close, but more of a pink beige. I at first thought they'd sent the wrong color, but there aren't any other colors that are even close. The color representation on the amazon page is not just a little off, but it just plain wrong! These gloves are nearly the same color as my skin tone, and not the brown I wanted. But, I decided the color wasn't bad enough to warrant a return. However, I wish I had just returned immediately.Second, I pulled the gloves out of the package, and looked at them, all over. I tested the stretch, looked at the fabric, and thought the fabric felt and looked cheap. I had hoped for better for $15. But, they are thin and soft and stretchy, so I put them on and walked out the door to go to work. By the time I got in my car and put my hands on the steering wheel, a big loop had been pulled out at the top of one thumb. I know it hadn't been there when I pulled it out of the packaging, and I hadn't done anything more strenuous than close and lock my door, walk down the stairs and get in my car. I was very disappointed. Upon looking again at the thumb opening, I could see the sticking was loose, poorly done and not adequate. I expect these gloves to unravel in a matter of days, without even going through the washing machine.Third, I am now at work, and have been typing on my computer for as long as it has taken to write this review, and my hands are begining to chill. I can feel the chill through the gloves. I'm not outside, and its not winter yet, and just starting to turn into fall, so temperatures here in Washington are not cold. Still, my hands aren't warm. I can say, the finger opening is right at the first knuckles of my fingers, so at the base of my fingers, so there is no interference with typing.I give these gloves two stars for texture, stretch and the ease of typing. However, the poor quality, the rapid unraveling, miss represented color and the lack of any real warmth subtracts three stars.Update, after wearing these for four hours, I am covered in lint and fuzz. I was talking to a couple people, looked down, and said "wow, I'm covered in fuzz". All those who I was talking too, said agreed immediately and said they'd noticed it as I walked up it was so bad, but didn't want to be the one to say something. These shed far worse than the bulky knitted gloves I was using before. They shed worse than anything I've ever worn! And, I have had three people in these last four hours tell me these look like ace bandages. They are exactly that color, NOT cocoa brown.

review: I have so many of these Geneva jelly watches now! I have purchased most of them here on Amazon, and every one of them has been in perfect condition upon arrival. This one, like the others I have purchased, has the larger face and band, comparable to a men's watch size. They do make a smaller face/band version that would be appropriate for kids or anyone with a smaller frame. Just be sure to check the item description to be sure you're getting the one you want!I have no complaints about the quality or durability of these watches. review: Sadly, I don't possess that kind of body.  The shirt ran small, and the fabric is VERY thin, so the slightest bulge in skin (eg, around a bra strap or something) is revealed in all its glory.  It shrinks a little when washed, but then stretches back out to its original size after wearing a few hours.  Note that it is "cardigan" in its design, but it is, in fact, a t-shirt.  A t-shirt with a cardigan neckline.  I mean, an ACTUAL cardigan neckline.  The model does not have the top buttons unbuttoned.  So, if you can get away with that kind of shirt, go for it and congratulations!  If you prefer not to have a neckline that plunging, don't get it.  It looks silly if worn over a tank top or camisole, and it looks silly if worn as an "open cardigan" over another shirt.  Mind you, these things don't stop me from wearing it, it's very comfortable, but impractical for a lot of situations.

review: These pants are really great for the price.The manufacturer has done their best to reproduce these pants to military specifications. That means draw bands on each side of the waist and drawstrings in the pantleg cuffs. Pants use buttons and not velcro or snaps with a zippered fly. A reinforced crotch and seat for rough wear. Most seams are double stitched.The material is a thicker material, more like winter BDU material than summer and is 55% cotton 45% poly which tells me its orgainc nature is breatheable but probably dries fast when wet due to being 45% poly. The lables even look milspec but the product is made in China. Lable also has detailed care and washing instructions.My pants came with Ultra Force metal dog tag and keychain on belt loop. Shipping was fast and arrived ahead of estimated delivery date.Although I have seen better made milspec pants, these are a definite buy for the price for short term use in the otdoors. If I were to be spending over a week at a time in the wilderness I might buy milspec at a higher price if I couldn't pack an extra pair of pants like these because the fabric doesn't look like it is of ripstop construction but I haven't put them to the test yet, they might be ripstop.All in all, I like these pants

review: I was extremely happy with this watch for the first two months of ownership.  It is very sturdy, looks nice, I *love* atomic feature where it sets the time itself.  It's easy to read, and the big light button on the front makes it really handy to do a quick time check in dark or low light.  I also like how the glass face is recessed so it prevents it getting scratched up.Now what I hate about it...  After 2 months or so, it stopped displaying the date and no loger showed a light when you hit the light button.  After doing some research I learned that this was a result of the solar part of its battery needed charging.  That was disappointing, so I left it on the window sill for a week with a nice southern exposure so it got a lot of light.  Sure enough, that did the trick and it now would show light and the date once again.  But then two days later it was drained again.  I repeated this several times (full charge, then use) and the same problem.  So I'm going to attempt sending it back for repair or replacement, but I'm not real happy.  And seeing how others have had similar problems, I would have to recommend getting a non-solar G-shock watch.  This one is just way more trouble than it's worth.

review: I ordered these to wear while motorcycle riding on hot, sunny days. I've gotten tired of having to wear a long sleeved shirt over my tank top or regular t-shirt. It keeps the sun and wind off, but I don't like the extra layer when it's hot.I ordered the small size on a Friday and held my breath that they'd get here before we left for our annual weekend motorcycle trip the following weekend. They arrived by Thursday, so they got here in plenty of time. I slipped them on to see how they fit and even though I was inside, they felt cool on my arms (I'd just come in from unloading groceries in 90 degree heat). They seem to fit fine without binding; I like that they cover my hands and extend well underneath a regular t-shirt sleeve. They might even work with a tank top with addition of a small piece of 2-sided tape. My arms are relatively skinny so I'll be interested to see if they continue to stay up over a long day of riding in sun and heat. I will update my review once they've been "road tested."EDIT:I was very happy with the performance of these sleeves. They stayed up all day, no problem with sagging or slipping down. They prevented sunburn and windburn on my arms and hands, and felt cool during all but the very, very hottest part of the day. (Sunny and 90+ degrees in mid-afternoon) Wetting them felt fabulous - other reviewers have said it feels like air conditioning and they're right. If only they'd stay wet for more than a few minutes on a moving motorcycle! Maybe I'll start carrying a small spray bottle with me for a quick cool-down when needed. (I'm a passenger, not driving!) It was nice not to have to mess with a long sleeve shirt. Off the bike, they were easy to slip off, roll up and tuck in my back pocket.
summary: Exactly what I was looking for

review: And branded as Under Armour.  I made two separate purchases of UA HeatGear boot socks in green about a month apart; the first batch of six pairs from US Cavalry (a reputable online retailer) and the second batch of six pairs from the military clothing sales in my BX.  The ones from US Cav are a darker, better looking shade of sage green and are VERY noticeably tighter at the top of the socks and stay up extremely well.  The ones from clothing sales are a faded green/grey color and the elastic at the top of the socks has no "rebound" so I'm constantly having to pull them back up when they ride down.  I'm not sure if anyone else has had this experience but it's disappointing that UA would put out a product where two batches of the same product are so vastly different.  I'd like to buy more of these socks as they are much better than anything else I've found out there but unless I can be assured of getting the darker, more elastic ones I'm going to stay away.EDIT: I purchased a third group of UA HeatGear boot socks from US Patriot Tactical online and they are a different shade of green than either of the previous two batches of socks I ordered/received from different sellers.  I contacted UA customer service about the discrepancies in color and fit and they were extremely apologetic and helpful but it doesn't change the fact that the HeatGear boot socks have serious quality control issues.  I still like the UA brand but I'm going to look for different socks for work.
summary: Seems like these are made by two different companies...

review: Lightweight boss. If you're looking for  heavy duty bulletproof carry on bag, this probably isn't it. But if your a professional traveler looking for something lightweight this is the piece/line you want. Its got thoughtful details for modular storage through out, it looks good, is well made and is very light. Do not confuse its lightweight with being cheaply made or cutting corners, that was definitely not the case. It reminds me of Tumi bags. But without the sell out feel they've come to have. This bag doesn't feel as rugged as Timbuk2 messenger bags, but so far after a trip to New Orleans, then NYC, then back to LA, as well as some car travel duty, there is not a single issue with it. I do wish the zipper pulls were a bit larger, but it does keep them from getting snagged. The other feature, or lack there of is that the wheels are straight line and not 360. I prefer straight as the bag doesn't move on you on the subway or inclines, but I know some people are ardent that 360 is the only way to go. Did I mention how good it looked? I even had someone ask me what kind of bag it was. Even having worked at a high end travel store as a summer job years ago I was surprised that some people noticed and cared so much. But I do enjoy that the logo is discreet enough that I don't instantly become a pick pocket target. Yes this bag is pricey, yes high quality lightweight luggage like this is worth the price. I just hope it continues to hold up.

This type of foam ('spacer foam'), is extremely thin, soft and pliable, meaning that it absolutely cannot and does not shape, mold or center heavier, voluminous breasts.  Furthermore, because the cups themselves provide zero support, the full weight of the breasts has to be carried by the band and its underwire. Painful!Despite several wearing and washings, this bra continued to be torture from a comfort perspective, and matronly from a fit perspective.  FYI, the size of the bra was correct (I was measured and I purchased at Nordstrom's).  I returned to Nordies and plan to try another Fantasie T shirt bra, likely here on Amazon.

review: I've never seen cloth quite like this. You can actually see right through a single layer of this material! I had wondered why they got such rave reviews but several people commented about them not lasting for long. Perhaps they were better quality material at one time and now Hanes is cutting corners. I can't imagine they'll last long. It's too bad as otherwise they look nice and fit well.Being a statistician, I decided to measure the difference between Hanes and the other brand I had around, Polo by Ralph Lauren. The Polo ones cost 3.7 times as much, so you'd expect better quality. It turns out that you're getting 23.4 grams of added material per pair with Polo (32.5% more, calculations below). The probability of this difference happening by chance is quite slim (0.008). Given the price difference, this may be reasonable. However the Hanes brand is so thin that I'm not sure they're better than not wearing any underwear at all.Note that in the measurements below, the Polo boxers were almost certainly from different batches measured six months to a year apart. The extra wear likely increased the variability in that brand. The Hanes were all from the same package and had been through the wash only twice.> Polo_Price  <- 38/3> Hanes_Price <- 17.10/5> (Price_Ratio <- Polo_Price / Hanes_Price)[1] 3.7037>> # Weight in grams for 5 pairs of each.> Polo  <- c(108,95,84,105,85)> Hanes <- c(74,72,71,73,70)> mean(Polo) - mean(Hanes)  # Mean difference[1] 23.4> mean(Polo) / mean(Hanes)  # Mean ratio[1] 1.325> t.test(Polo,Hanes)Welch Two Sample t-testdata:  Polo and Hanest = 4.6837, df = 4.163, p-value = 0.008548alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 095 percent confidence interval:9.741 37.059sample estimates:mean of x mean of y95.4  72.0

review: I purchased this for my petite 17 year old God daugher for Christmas 2012. I have not given it to her yet as it is still November of 2012; however, I did get it out of the package and checked it out. As other reviewers have noted, it does run small....which is precisely why I chose this Hat....as Molly, who is 17 is very small and petite for her age, so I wanted something small so it would fit. Just by stretching it, eyeballing it, trying to put it securely on my head, seeing if it would fit me nicely, I determined that it was just the right size for her. It comes no where close to fitting me properly, and I have a relatively small head for an adult female. I cannot recall the exact size as I am not a "hat" person myself, but the smallest standard women's size fits me perfectly. This sits way up high on my head and my ears show and it looks rediculous. It will, though, look just lovely on Molly who likes things like Angora, pink and hats with flowers, even if they are the same color as the hat. I found her some nice, feminine pink angora gloves to go with the hat and she should be set for her walk to school (God forbid in today's day and age that she have to walk,,,where IS the child's car? I am rather tongue in cheek!)Soft Pink Angora Knit Gloves With Silver Buttons.I think she will like these, but if she does not, I have until Jan 31 to return them which is quite nice of Amazon. So I am happy with the hat because it runs small; I found gloves to go with it; and all can be returned if needed. Heck of a deal.But, if you are looking at this as a regular woman's hat, I would purchase it only if your are a very petite lady. It is beautiful....but as so many beautiful pieces of clothing are....it is only for a small princess, not a large one!

review: This is really just OKAY. I wear Levis 505 straight leg jeans, mostly. They aren't low rise, but also not wicked high mom jeans. I tried the bellaband with them, and it just felt like my pants were unbuttoned. I couldn't get comfortable in it. Maybe for women who wear low rise this would work better.I also have hips and a small waist. I always felt like this was riding up and sagging down at the same time. I know it sounds weird, but I actually feel like as I get further along (I'm 16 weeks and have been trying to use this since I was 8 weeks) that it may work better. My butt and thighs are actually slimming down, so as my belly gets bigger I may be able to wear my jeans comfortably, as long as the bellaband will stay up.I also look way less pregnant when wearing this with regular jeans. I'd rather just wear maternity jeans. Much more comfortable.
summary: Not really for higher pants or big hips

review: These Clarks Indigo sandals have an up-to-date design, great leather work, superb color (deep orange), and a perfect fit--except for the footbed.  Unlike every other pair of Clarks sandals I have purchased and worn for years, these mysteriously have no arch. I knew this before I bought them after reading the reviews, so I thought I could probably get some transparent arch supports to compensate for the lack.  I did finally find some that work, but it was difficult to find any thick enough to make any difference. Because of the arch problem, I deducted two stars as I consider this a serious design flaw.  Had the footbed had a better design I would have given the sandals 5 stars.A note on the height:  This sandal looks and feels about a half inch taller than it looks in the pictures.  As the design is more casual looking than a dressy sandal, a slightly lower heel would make the shoe more practical for daily casual or business use--but that's just a personal preference.The velcro closures are really unnoticeable, and they function to help in getting the sandals on and off; like huaraches, they are a little  tricky to get on but hug your feet once they're on. The 8-1/2 fit me exactly true to size.The sandals look great on the foot and are very flattering to legs and feet.So:  I have kept my arch-flawed but gorgeous sandals.

review: Champion used to make a great sports bra, and they were my favorite for years.  Sadly, they changed the style and I changed to Moving Comfort.I need good support at 36DD, and this bra provides it and is relatively cool.  I own 3 and they have been worn and washed many times.  I wash them by hand or in a lingerie bag in the machine, and always drip dry.  Problem is, the band definitely curls up in front, as they wear.It is a bit shear in the cup, but "No Peek" covers by Wacoal, that you just slip inside each cup solves the problem.  (You'll find them here on Amazon or at Nordstom). They stay in place, are easily washed, and no one could tell you're wearing them.  Best of all, I can't tell I'm wearing them.Just bought a Wacoal sports bra, but I'm already not liking the fact that it goes practically up to my collar bones (I'm exaggerating of course) in the front.  I may return it.  Still searching for a sports bra that will support, lift, and separate, but doesn't make me sweat to death and feel like I'm wearing an orthopedic product...

Firstly the leather was only so so quality.  It felt a bit thin and papery.  The color is gorgeous and with good care it would deepen wonderfully to a lovely honey color.  Secondly the bag has hardly any depth.  This was a deal breaker for me as I like a roomy bag.  Why is was made so thin I don't know.  Thirdly the strap system can get a bit twisted.  It isn't an instantaneous conversion from shoulder strap to backpack mode as you need to carefully feed the straps or untwist them.  Really wanted to love but It's going back :(

review: Bottom line: if you like really big watches then consider this one.Not just big but heavy as well. It weighs over half a pound. The band is half again as wide as a normal band and as thick as most watches. The watch proper is over 1/2 inch thick.There *is* a unidirectional rotating bezel. Unfortunately, it's next to worthless. The markings are black raised numbers on the black rubber ring and very difficult to read.The band is not adjustable. You need to remove links if you want to shorten it. (You will want to shorten it unless you have huge wrists.A sign of attempted quality was the included "Invicta Certificate of Quality Control." That's where "Tom" checked off all the things he checked on the watch back on 9/28/11. Too bad he missed that the hand's didn't line up.The watch comes with a 1-year warranty. If it breaks all you have to do is pay to get it to the Invicta service facility and include a Money Order for $25 to cover its return. I don't know if that's good or bad and thought it was at least worth mentioning.There is some good news - This is likely to be a unique timepiece. I can't imagine you will run into many other people wearing won. I guarantee that people will notice you though.Final thought... There's a quote from "Friends" where Chandler, referring to an absurdly large ID bracelet he received as a gift says, " It slowed it down for little while but once I got used to the weight I was back on track."Final, final thoughts: Invicta is NOT a Swiss company. The name used to be Swiss and was bought by a company operating out of Florida. Invicta has a tradition of assigning randomly large retain prices on their products. This watch is worth... well, like anything it's worth what someone will pay for it and I'd wager not many people would pay the suggested retail price for this or any of their timepieces.
summary: Barely OK, at a drastically reduced price, *if* you like really big watches

review: Very nifty tool and does what it says. Paid for itself the first day I had it. In less than ten minutes after I had it out of the shipping carton, I had snapped open the backs of two watches that had been sitting on my dresser for several years collecting dust. Even Walmart has gotten to where they don't won't want to change out batteries unless you purchased the watch from them AND have a receipt to prove it. Taking a timepiece to a watch shop or jewelry store will run you about $10 to $15 just for a new battery. Better and cheaper just to buy the tools and do it yourself. In just a few days, I was able to get both a pocketwatch and wristwatch up and going again with this tool. The case opener itself seems quite sturdy and well made.PLEASE NOTE: If you have a water-proof or water-resistant watch, this tool MAY not work. This device failed on my old Seiko, but only because it has the screw-type case. For those watches, you'll need the special wrench for the rear bezel. summary: One of the best investments I've made.

review: I am an avid North Face fan, and own many of their products. I decided to invest in this little beauty about a year ago. This jacket provides warmth and style in addition to serving dual purposes. This is a great stand alone jacket in temperatures as low as 35 degrees when paired with a long sleeve shirt and makes a great mid layer when paired with an insulated jacket. The Polartec materiel is the best thing about this fleece, soft to the touch and sturdy. It has stood the test of time through about a year of washes/wear and still looks brand new. The materiel does not show any signs of wear unlike the Denali's cheaper alternatives. The abrasion panels on the shoulders and elbows add a stylish touch while increasing the durability of the jacket. The price seems a little hefty to the average consumer, but compared to similar products by other brands such as Patagonia or Arcteryx it's a bargain. Overall this is a great jacket for the intended purpose..... a fleece jacket. I often hear complaints that it is not water proof or as warm as a winter coat, and that's because it is not a rain coat or an insulated jacket it is a FLEECE jacket. Although technology has come quite a long way we are still not there yet, but if and when there is a breakthrough in said technology The North Face will be the first to yield the results. Until then this is the best jacket in it's class.

review: I've recently gone up in bra size and found that the Victoria's Secret bras I've been loyally purchasing no longer cut the mustard.  It didn't fit right to me, even when I bought the right size.  I was also looking for a wireless bra that could not only offer adequate support for larger breasts, but also have straps that didn't dig into my shoulders.  I always had to give up one for the other.  I decided to try these.  After putting them on for the first time, I immediately realized why I've never bought department store brand underwear.  These, quite frankly, are ugly.  I did not want to look like my mother, but these bras give you that matronly 70's torpedo boob effect.  They also run a little on the larger side, I think.  What was the right size for me in every other brand is a little on the looser side with this brand.  The coverage is excellent, but I feel like an old woman.  The straps are so comfy, but even with a tight camisole under my blouse, I have these pointy boobs.  They're making me kinda self conscious at work.  I will most definitely not be wearing these when my boyfriend comes over.  These are very much the "granny panties" of the bra world, and that's such a shame because they're sooo comfy and provide the support I need all without wires.  I'll be wearing these around the house for sure, but I don't think I'll be wearing these out anymore.  I'm way too young to be wearing my mom's undergarments.  I chose to give it a middle-of-the-road score because it really is a comfy bra that does what it says, but what good is that when you're too self conscious to wear them past your front porch?
summary: Definitely NOT a Date Night Bra.....

review: Well...I'm a bit disappointed with my purchase of these TOMS shoes. I was really looking forward to getting them. My son has a pair and loves them, and I thought they looked pretty comfy, so I took the plunge and read up on TOMS shoes. I loved their mission to outfit children in need with shoes and that *my* purchase of a pair would put a pair of shoes on a child without really touched my heart.I picked out these cute navy umbrella stripe shoes in a size 6.5. I have never really had a fitting issue with any shoes ever. I always buy a 6.5 when purchasing online because my normal size is a 6 and I give a 1/2 size up just in case something runs small.These are somewhat small for me. My right foot is snug, but nothing uncomfortable and I know with that shoe if I wore it a bit it would eventually break in. However, the left shoe is SO snug that it actually HURTS my heel to wear it, and walking is painful in it. It feels like it's pushing my heel into the shoe, not a good thing when you have Plantar Fasciitis and have heel pain already. I did like the support inside the shoes, the design, the color etc. But the odd fit was disappointing. I do know that someone can have a foot that is slightly bigger than the other, but mine are not that much different as I've had my feet measured professionally for shoes to wear when my Plantar Fasciitis is really bad, so it's the SHOE not my feet. One is NOTICEABLY smaller than the other.I don't know if I would buy TOMS again. I love their mission, but I just couldn't wear their shoes.

review: Update: I've only worn these around the house but already I notice threads coming undone. I have no idea what it means long term. And since Vasque won't return my messages it appears they won't get fixed either, warranty or not.------------------------------------------------Shoes are hard to buy let alone something like a boot where fit can be vital to survival. And it seems that everyone's feet are different, even between the two we have. My feet are a bit wide, the right being wider then the left, of course. It's hard to find hiking boots that come in different widths and trying to fit into "regular" can be hell. Even though these come in different widths I still went a half size larger for better comfort.I haven't hiked in these yet but after wearing them several days, several hours a day, for everyday wear they don't bother my feet at all. With the little extra room I could wear a heavy sock for those cold days.The tread on these is not that great and is a hard, stiff rubber. Just walking on our back, pebbled patio that was drenched from winter's squalls and buildup of muck from runoff, I was not able to get a good grip and was literally sliding around even on the rough surface.I have winter/snow boots that don't leak at all but are too warm for the other seasons but I still wanted the Gortex waterproof these claim for those times when you are hit with rain or even snow during the warmer months. But I am concerned about the breathable mesh and have read that the previous version of these leaked.I tried to contact Vasque but immediately got a message machine. Is this an out of home company? The message said to visit their website and fill out the online support request form. I did and no response.This is my first pair of Vasque shoes/boots and I've only had them about a month. We'll see how well these boots work over time and if there is anyone home.
summary: Best fit for me and comfortable but not great quality

review: I have big boobs. There, I said it.I have such big boobs, in fact, that the largest cup size (DDD) of this particular model is just a teensy bit too small to hold in my particular sweater puppies. But I buy them anyway because Bali got it right with this one; in fact I've gone through several because they're the only bras I wear.The under-wires, though they will eventually break (it's inevitable if you are blessed with enormous melons), are comfortable, which is hard to be if you're an underwire.The cups themselves are soft and cozy, and there are no seams - no bullet bras here!The straps are padded where they should be, as opposed to way too far in front like some of the more "just add more fabric to the cup" types of extra-endowed bras out there...those folks don't realize that the massive mammaries drag the bra *forward*...which means if you leave the straps the same as for the B-cuppers, those half-conceived padded bits will be somewhere on the front of the shoulder, doing nobody any good at all.Lastly, you should know this is NOT a minimizer. At all. And why should it be?! Be PROUD of your assets! Don't strap em' in like they did something wrong! Let them be round and bouncy and pleasant for all to behold, including you. Grab one of these accurately-sized bras (measure yourself first, check online for the way to do it properly) and a v-neck t-shirt, and let em' be happy hooters.The only thing I wish for this bra would be a few more cup sizes, and more color... if I could, I'd buy this bra in every color of the rainbow. I'd buy two of each, in fact, and some for friends as well.

review: Our hospital has gone to the "uniform color by profession" policy.  Meaning, I'm forced to wear one solid color each and every day.  The only form of self-expression we are allowed are our socks and shoes. I've been wearing funky socks for years, but these shoes are just what I was searching for. They are a bit heavy, but you get used to that very quickly, they have a small heel, which I find helpful for reaching stuff on higher shelves. I tried some on locally before ordering them because I wasn't sure about the size.  I decided to buy mine a half size lager because they felt pretty tight in the store.  They do stretch out a bit, but it takes a few weeks.  They are extremely comfortable and I wear them 13-14 hours per shift. The pattern is perfect because I'm a knitter and it allows me to bring a part of my love for knitting to work.  The finish is shiny and smooth, which allows you to wipe any unwanted fluids off your shoes- very important in my profession!  I will be buying more Danskos in the future!

review: the material is nice, thick and soft.  the pockets are deep but a hole opened up in the seam when i put my hand in.  it will be easy to fix.  from reading the other reviews, it seems that the sewing is not of high quality.  I am a female and wanted open bottom sweatpants and it's cheaper to buy mens' sometimes so i got this pair.  I am 5'3" and 120 pounds, 27 waist, 39" hips - the small just barely gets around my hips.  it's comfortable after i stretch it out.  even after a day of wear, the pull has pulled the pockets open.  The rise, the length from waist to crotch seam is really really long!  i have to fold the top of the pants over.  i fold the legs up an inch but that is about right for my height.  I wear a lot of men's small pants so I have a basis for comparison.  These pants are long in the leg and long in the rise; narrow in the thigh and hips.  for the price, yeah go for it.

review: Firstly, I am writing this based on the shoes performance as a water shoe.I see everyone saying order big, but my guess is these are people wearing socks with these, and using it as a normal sneaker.I ordered my normal size but when wearing sockless in the water (what it's meant for) they were a bit too long and I could tell right away that the heel movement would cause blisters.I ended up going a half-size smaller than my normal fit, and they are perfect.  I've worn them all-day, fishing/creek-walking with some light hiking thrown in and have yet to get a blister.  Again, no socks.The nice part is between the material being so stretchy, and the adjustable straps, it's not an issue to wear light socks with them still, if/when you want to.  I have a few times since they look nice and sporty as a normal sneaker as well.  The fold-down heel feature is REALLY nice too. They are super comfortable when worn that way, and basically turn into slippers!Traction in the water on slippery rocks is adequate.The only two cons I have to report are:1-they get stinky when worn all-day barefoot, but just wash them out and they're fine after drying.2-the front toe-protector should go a tiny bit higher.  Be sure your toenails aren't too long.  I could see a big-toe poking through the mesh.I bought these primarily for wearing when fishing along streams and rivers in the warm weather, and wanted something with a sturdy enough sole to be able to do some hiking as well. So far these more than fit the bill, and with the current sale price you really can't beat them.I ended up buying two pairs so I'd have a backup for when the first pair wears out.Just be sure to use caution regarding sizing as I said.  Everyone says they run small and to order a size larger, but they are probably wearing thick hiking socks with them or something...

review: I want to thank the previous reviewers for noting to size up. Yes, do size up. I was pant size 4-6 in Gap pre-pregnancy, currently at 20 weeks with twins, having gained 15 pounds so far and the medium (on the smallest setting) is just the right fit. I can't imagine squeezing into Small.It is comfortable, especially when standing and moving around, and pretty much invisible under clothes.The biggest issue that I have with it is that it has only 3 sets of hooks (=3 adjustments). I feel that the velcro style would be way more flexible in that respect and I may get a second velcro band or investigate sewing more rows of hooks onto this one. For example, right now I feel that I'd rather have it somewhere between 1 and 2nd sets of hooks, because if feels a little tight on the 1st set and a little loose on the 2nd.
