review: and so am I.  I LOVE this hat.  No, it's not going to have the degree of 'slouch' that hangs down the back of your neck, that the youngsters are looking for, today, but it has a mild slouch that works for me.  And, I have a big head size!  I have always hoped it meant more brains, since it surely has meant I have had a hard time finding hats that fit (and I love hats), but I'm having my doubts.  This appears to be one thing I share with Oprah, as she complains about this issue, often. Oh why couldn't it have equated to that level of success. Oy vey!Kidding aside, I have just ordered this hat in black from the same seller, Luxury Divas.  They were previously so prompt with their delivery and care of product, that I didn't look elsewhere.  And, even though we are coming to the end of winter here in TX, I wanted to make sure I had another, in case they were discontinued.  I'm one who believes in stocking up on items that are keepers, considering the number of disappointments we all encounter.  Okay, that can equal boredom, but who cares.  Comfort (and style) comes first!  Besides personal trademarks have always been on trend.I like the rosy pink of this hat.  I think it would compliment most complexions.  I have found a scarf that will work well with it, so it looks lovely.  The black, I've just ordered,  will be a 'throw-on' for almost anything, so I'm glad to have secured it. It will look great with my black leather bomber jacket.  It almost makes me long for winter again --- not!I'm really pleased with this purchase, as has been the response of many reviewers. If you're okay with having a thick, warm, well constructed hat, with mild to moderate slouch, this is the hat for you. I like that I can style it, so I don't look flat-headed.  That doesn't work for me.If they are still selling this product next fall, I might opt for additional colors, too.  It doesn't get too cold here in TX, but the wind the northers produce will rip from one ear, right through the other.  And, I can pull this one over my ears and it still looks stylish.

review: ...but you get what you pay for, no?This item came quickly enough, faster than expected to be honest, but I was disappointed when I opened the package as the quality of the locket seemed cheap and a little on the flimsy side.  I was looking for a locket with a little more substance to it, a little more weight. I finally settled on this one because of the price...if it wasn't quite what I was looking for (size-wise and quality), then for the price no harm, no foul.  I got what I paid for.The locket is on the small side, thin and difficult to open and I'm afraid that too many repeated openings will break it because the metal seems so thin.  The etched design on the front is slightly rough to the touch as well, though not enough to be annoying while worn.  I only received it this afternoon and have been wearing it ever since, trying to make up my mind whether to keep it or return it.  While the locket is large enough to put pictures in, the pictures would end up being so small it really wouldn't be worth the fight to get them set.The only good point of this is the chain, it seems sturdy and I like the fact that it's a rolo chain.  However, at 18", it sits way to high on me (the heart sits just below the base of my throat).  I should have purchased a longer chain.Like I said, for the price, it's okay, but it definitely isn't what I was looking for.

review: The bra is ok, it does its job but it's far from perfect. I have a list of things that I like about it but I wouldn't buy it again. The list of good qualities can be found in other nursing bras, so the things that I don't like about this bra count too much against buying it again or recommending it to someone else.Pros:- Very soft material, comfortable in the skin.- It looks sturdy and durable and the materials seem of good quality, but I don't know yet because it is new.- No wires: more comfort ant it doesn't block milk flow.- The cup is thick enough that it doesn't show a lot (nipples or nursing pads), but also soft.Cons:- It fits too big, I have an extra room that annoys me every time I use it.- It is difficult to fold the flap down completely, it stays on the way and my baby has to fight it to nurse comfortably. I hate that. It seems to have something to do with the side boning, but I haven't got into it yet to try and solve this problem.- The latches are somewhat difficult to unsnap one handed until you get the hang of it. To close them they have two "stages", so make sure you closed them completely after nursing.Overall, for the price, it is a good bra, but I would definitely try something else next time I buy a nursing bra.UPDATE:Not happy with the bra. The more I use it, the more I feel what others complain about the side boning. The more you fold the flap down it starts to lose shape and then it starts to hurt. I have to fold it inwards (if that makes sense at all) in order to try to prevent the boning from deforming and hurting more. Another thing is that after a few washes, the material inside the cup is starting to show through the soft fabric that covers it. So the look and feel are not totally smooth anymore and this shows in some thin fabric tops. I'm downgrading my review from three to two stars because I don't know for how long I will bare to use this bra or for how long it will hold up.
summary: Ok, but I would look for another bra next time I buy a nursing bra

review: Ordered two of these skirts in beige and black, hoping to get something comfortable to wear around the house this summer. They are pretty comfortable; the fabric is very soft, the colors are true, they are well sewn and they are the right length, but the pros are far outweighed by the con's.First, they are very thin. The black isn't as bad as the beige, but I can still see through it in bright light. The beige is completely sheer. I could count the freckles on my thighs through the fabric without stretching it at all. They are also far too tight through the hips. I ordered a large instead of a medium to avoid having to worry about showing a panty line, but I probably would have had to order an XL or XXL to avoid it. The waistband is so tight that the waistband seam itself looks like a panty line.I wanted to return them, but it would have cost me what I paid for them to ship them back to LA from NY state, so I'm stuck with them. Fortunately I'll be able to use them anyway. I need a long half slip to wear under an ankle length dress I bought for a wedding I'm attending next month, and the beige will work for that. The black will work as a strapless swim coverup when my family visits a water park this summer. Eventually, I may sew the two together with the seams touching to make them into one reversible skirt.Two stars, the skirts look cute and are well made, but seriously disappoint, and in my opinion fall very short of the advertising.
summary: How are they disappointing? Let me count the ways...

review: I wear sandals 12 months out the year, and up until recently I have worn Columbia Men's Tango Thong full time. The Tangos only shortcoming was that it was a not a good sandal when it got wet, due to the fabric insole. So I decided to try something different with a rubber insole, The Sperry Santa Cruz. My review will compare the two.Sizing: I wear a 10.5 in most everything, and a size 11 was a perfect fit. I have a semi wide foot that the Tango's suited well and the Santa Cruz's are slightly narrower sandal. Edge to the TangoStyle: Of course very subjective, but I give the edge to the Santa Cruz. The boat shoe like style is too my liking.Comfort: This is a major category for me. Im in sandals almost every day year round. From walking all day in them to fishing in them, Im just a sandal guy. The only time I wear shoes is at work. The Tango has a cushier insole and more arch support then the Santa Cruz. The Tango's strap is also slightly more comfortable. The initial break-in of the Santa Cruz's led to irritated skin along the strap. The Tango's strap is of a softer material, while the Santa Cruz is tougher. Walking extended periods of time they are about the same, my feet never get fatigued in either.Durability/Functionality: One of the main reasons I decided to try soething different was the poor wet performance due to its fabric insole. It would become water logged and mushy to walk in. It was also harder to clean for the same reason. The Santa Cruz, with it's rubber insole, doesn't have this problem, yet still is comfortable and supportive. Second reason for moving away from the Tango's was that both pairs I had, the thin portion of the strap pulled out the sole. Not the whole thing, just the fabric overlay. They still functioned but looked silly. The sole of the Tango was thicker, hence would keep your foot dry when stepping in puddles and such, almost like a sole of an trail shoe. The Santa Cruz isn't as thick and its treads are not near as aggressive for releasing mud/debris.Overall, if I didn't need a sandal that could perform while wet, I would of stayed with the Tango's. However, the Santa Cruz offers similar comfort with increased functionality, but has it has drawbacks as well.  Ideally I would have a pair of both, I would wear the Tango for everything except wet situations.
summary: Best all around Sandal, except when wet

review: The directions were on a quarter sheet of paper, kind of crumpled up in the box. Although the watch looks cool, you can't set it at all!The directions WILL NOT HELP YOU, unless you are fluent in broken english. I did manage to set the time, but it is impossible to set the alarm. It's not simple, like pressing the mode button a couple times, hitting the light button to select what you want, and pressing that last button to change the minutes/hours. There is no navigating the watch, and somehow, I turned the hourly beep alarm on and it's incredible loud and it won't turn off. Since I spend the majority of my day in lecture halls and labs in college, I can't wear this in public because it's so loud and distracting. The couple times I did wear it before I messed up the hourly beep, I did get compliments though - it definitely looks cool.The only other good thing is the band. I would pay the $9 for the band alone without the watchpiece. It's thick and very durable, and fits perfectly around my wrist. I need to figure out how to take it off and put it on my other watch before I throw this annoying thing away.If I could manage to figure out how to set this watch properly, I may just wear it around, but it's too frustrating and not worth my time considering I have a million other perfectly capable watches that aren't such a pain to set. I've also noticed that as it sits in my desk, the clock gets pretty far behind where it's supposed to be, much quicker than I'd expect it to.

review: Once upon a time, Russell was a good quality manufacturer of athletic wear but they've gotten cheap like everyone else.  The waists run very big on these pants, which is contrary to previous versions, and the drawstrings are much flimsier - I can see some people breaking them if they pull too hard.  The stitching on the insides of the waists is very sparse and spaced far apart, and you're able to see some of the waistband material after a few washes - I do foresee these pants falling apart in a relatively short time because I see holes by the pockets after six weeks.  The pant legs are extremely long and people will step on the back of the pants and ruin them.  The crotch area is VERY long and you have to wear these up to your armpits to get the crotch right and keep the pant legs from dragging.  I believe these were a labeling mistake by Russell, that they're considered a tall/long, and they sold them for pennies on the dollar to Amazon - Amazon, in turn, doesn't say anything either and sells them for full price as regular length pants for amazing profit percentage - think about it - the legs are WAY too long, the crotch is WAY too long, and the waists are WAY too big - they both benefit - Russell gets rid of mistakes and Amazon makes a killer profit.  Have stranger things happened?  Sure they have ...
summary: Waist Runs Big - Sparse Stitching in the Waistband - Holes Develop Quickly - Crotch Too Long

review: To start off, I am a competitive runner who runs 40-60 miles a week, ranging from sprints to long distance. I saw a commercial for these shoes and thought the same thing that a lot of people probably thought: "Oh boy, Reebok is just trying to make a pathetic attempt to imitate the Nike Free TR Fit Womens' Sneaker Style# 429785-400." The commercial was well put together, so I decided to research these shoes and quickly discovered that making these shoes was no easy task. It was apparent that Reebok hired experts to design the shoes and put a hefty amount of time into making sure that these shoes were safe for running on trails, concrete, and any other surface that running would have to endure.I have never owned a pair of Nike Free Runs or any shoe that is so low to the ground. Before the Realflex shoes, I ran in a pair of Nike Zoom Victory+, which allowed for an easy transition into these shoes because both are very lightweight. I have owned five pairs of them so these were the first different pair of training shoes that I have bought in about two years.I went to a local store to learn more about these shoes. The store had a sample pair sitting outside for viewing and to examine the shoe. As seen in many of the pictures, the Realflex shoe is very flexible and can be bent to form a full circle, which I didn't really understand the purpose of at first. I have never owned a pair of Nike Free Runs or any shoe that is low to the ground quite like these. The Realflex boasts of a shoe with ample comfort yet lower to the ground than any shoe in its class, allowing for a more natural run. I had a salesperson get me a pair of the Realflex shoes, and half of a size lower than my actual foot size turned out to be the right fit. My first thought was "Wow, these shoes give something back!" I've never tired on a pair of shoes that felt like they've had anywhere near as much spring as these. I jogged around the store and the shoes felt simply amazing. The flexibility of the shoes can really be noticed when standing on ones toes, so these  shoes are great for both sprinting (on the toes), or easy distance (midfoot or heel).The only reason why I didn't give these shoes a five star rating concerns the heel of the shoe. The part of the shoe where the Achilles Tendon meets the shoe has a ripple in the fabric. I asked for another pair and it had the same problem, so I assume that most, if not all, of the Realflex shoes have this flaw. Luckily, the ripple flattened out in about 4 hours of wearing and was no problem at all. If you go to the store and feel the same problem in the heel, I urge you to ignore it any buy the shoes anyway, knowing that it will subside in no time at all.Now I understand that everybody is not a hardcore runner, but I do think that this shoe is beneficial for just about anybody that runs. A runner who puts in a lot of miles each week will appreciate this shoe because it is lightweight and a comfortable shoe. On the other hand, a moderate runner will appreciate this shoe because they're not luggin around an extra 8oz on each foot, which is the actual weight of some of the heavier running shoes.To conclude, this shoe is a great deal. For a relatively low price, you are purchasing some of the most advanced technology in any running shoe ever created. This shoe is great for just about any runner, and provides great cushioning and flexibility for the runner. My advice to you is this: buy this shoe!!!
summary: Great new technology translates to an awesome running shoe!

many of the pictures the realflex shoe is very flexible and can be bent to form a full circle which i didnt really understand the purpose of at first i have never owned a pair of nike free runs or any shoe that is low to the ground quite like these the realflex boasts of a shoe with ample comfort yet lower to the ground than any shoe in its class allowing for a more natural run i had a salesperson get me a pair of the realflex shoes and half of a size lower than my actual foot size turned out to be the right fit my first thought was wow these shoes give something back ive never tired on a pair of shoes that felt like theyve had anywhere near as much spring as these i jogged around the store and the shoes felt simply amazing the flexibility of the shoes can really be noticed when standing on ones toes so these shoes are great for both sprinting on the toes or easy distance midfoot or heelthe only reason why i didnt give these shoes a five star rating concerns the heel of the shoe the part of the shoe where the achilles tendon meets the shoe has a ripple in the fabric i asked for another pair and it had the same problem so i assume that most if not all of the realflex shoes have this flaw luckily the ripple flattened out in about hours of wearing and was no problem at all if you go to the store and feel the same problem in the heel i urge you to ignore it any buy the shoes anyway knowing that it will subside in no time at allnow i understand that everybody is not a hardcore runner but i do think that this shoe is beneficial for just about anybody that runs a runner who puts in a lot of miles each week will appreciate this shoe because it is lightweight and a comfortable shoe on the other hand a moderate runner will appreciate this shoe because theyre not luggin around an extra oz on each foot which is the actual weight of some of the heavier running shoesto conclude this shoe is a great deal for a relatively low price you are purchasing some of the most advanced technology in any running shoe ever created this shoe is great for just about any runner and provides great cushioning and flexibility for the runner my advice to you is this buy this shoe

I wanted the style like this that has an even band around the front, sides and back, making the whole thing like an upside down, small pot (minus the brim). The circle part on top of the head pulls out of shape because of the strange, short front-piece over the brow.The sewing, buckle and fabric were fine. It fit my head I'm a woman, 5'6". review: I have both the regular fleece-lined Mammoth clog and the Woolly Mammoth. The difference in fit (with the same sizes) is very noticeable. While my foot has plenty of room (even a bit too much) in the regular Mammoth, it fits snuggly into the Woolly Mammoth. It is quite tight with regular socks, although I even followed the recommendation to order a size up. Wearing thicker winter socks, I am unable to fit my feet into these. One star deduction for that because I feel that with having ordered one size up, I should be able to wear thicker socks AND fit into the shoes.That said, here is what I like about the Woolly Mammoths: I love the adjustable heel strap. As a matter of fact these were the reason I bought these clogs in the first place. It makes for much better and stable walking. Also helpful in colder weather: the fewer holes on top of the shoe as well as a much better-profiled sole. I imagine this shoe to get better traction when it is slippery outside.Just wish these came in more color selections! I bought the black with the gray lining but feel it is a bit drab...(but hated the other colors even more so finally settled for this combo).The only other comment I have is that I am not sure the heel strap and the somewhat different sole warrant a $20 higher price. But all in all I am happy and am sure I will be wearing these a lot this coming winter.

review: Let me start by saying that this bag is extremely attractive.  It is also very large and heavy, which I wouldn't mind if it was functional.  Unfortunately, the bag has serious design flaws that really limit how much stuff you can put in it.  The leather on this bag is extremely stiff -- no give at all -- and the opening is too narrow.  Even when I pull the opening apart as far as it will go, I have to squeeze my hand down into it.  My laptop fits, but just barely and I can't carry anything else at the same time.  Without the laptop, I can carry one file folder about half full.  The front pocket has the same problem -- it doesn't open wide enough to be useful.  If you are wondering, I am a youngish woman with very slim hands, so the problem isn't me -- it is the bag.  I am returning this item because I can't justify carrying such a big, heavy bag if it doesn't function the way I need it to.

review: Ballet slippers are my go to footwear around the house. I've purchased dozens over the years and wear them to rags.  I can't say that Isotoner is my favorite brand (the best I found was Robinsons May Charter Club but haven't been able to find them since RM was purchased by Macy's) but they are far better than most.I just purchased the black version of these classic terry Isotoners (model number 9896H).  The terry fabric keeps my feet warm but it is "breathable" so my feet stay comfortable.  Isotoner has one of the thickest most padded soles I've found which means I can wear them comfortably all day.  In addition, the sole is firm and has some treading so I don't feel like I'm slipping on our hard wood floors.The one negative I have is that this pair runs small.  I wear a size 8 shoe and always buy Isotoner L(8/9).  While this pair is OK, they are very snug and my toe goes all the way to the end.  No way could a size 9 wear these.  Glancing at other reviews, there appears to be quite a disparity in sizing results.  My experience with buying shoes on Amazon is that just selecting a different color can effect the quality and sizing.  Looking closely at the product pictures you can see some of the difference.  The white slippers look like another pair I have with terry like a soft, fluffy bath towel.  But the black, which I bought, looks (and is) like the flat terry of a beach towel. I wish everyone would be specific about the size and color they bought because it can make a difference in results.I would recommend that if you buy the black, go one size up.
summary: Not all Isotoners are created equal

review: I reviewed these before, but that was when I had just gotten another pair of shoes, so my review was biased.  Here's a more objective review.I'm 30, devastatingly handsome and well proportioned...lol.  NOT  lifelong runner; I fell in love with running while in the Army Infantry, and we did a lot.  I run anywhere from 2-10 miles at a time, up to 20mi/week.  I try to run a 5/10k every month, but my gf makes me feel guilty if she doesn't go with, so I never get the chance to let loose and shine!SHOES: great shoes.  Wish I cold stop thereNew Balance Minimus MR10 are designed with foot conscious runners in mind.Seamless, mesh upper gives great flex while not being too loose.  Outsoles are cushy with less than 1/2" of material.  I'd prefer not running on styrofoam inserts you find in TV boxes.The shoe itself is incredibly pliable, you could wrap it around itself with ease, much like the Nike Free, but easier, almost like a sock with a thick sole and awesome red color!  The upper is a seamless one piece work of art which is breathable, not too flexible, and not too constrictive.  The toe box is wonderfully accommodating, and THIS IS SOMETHING I REALLY APPRECIATE IN RUNNING SHOES.  The heel is fairly narrow, but fits my foot well, without much stiffness.Vibram "pods" are "strategically" placed on the bottom, and after a year haven't worn out a bit.The tongue is part of the upper; it comes across from the instep into a wide, non flexible flap that stays in place across the outer part of the top of the foot.  Apparently this is a new addition from previous models.  I have no experience with those but I like the tongue on these.  I WEAR A 9.5, which is true to size.  However, in some others (Nike Free) I wear a 10.  I transitioned into these during my barefoot Five Finger phase.  It was a welcome transition.  the shoe has a minimal 4mm drop (compared to the 2mm drop on the mizuno wave universe 5, which I also just bought :)).  The arch and heel portions of the sole are rather narrow, which isn't really noticeable when running.  The shoes certainly make for a somewhat uncomfortable ride when on rocky terrain, but I avoid those when possible, or I suck it up ;).  Speaking of soles, I'd say these compare to the Nike free 3.0, but a bit more thin, and more "whole", as opposed to sliced and diced into tiny flexy squares.RUNNING: When I run, I have no idea how far I'm about to go, i just go.  These shoes have carried me well from quick dog walks to my more frequent and recent 15k's.  the "lack" of cushioning isn't noticeable and is welcome when I'm focusing on how my feet feel when they hit the ground.  The shoes can be a bit loose if not tightened well, which translates to movement of the toes and subsequently blisters, especially on longer runs or when cross training.  I prefer to get away from these on distances > 10k due to the pain I feel from running with a more forward strike, but that's more my own style, others may or may not have the same problem.I've taken these on a few 5k races, and they performed a little poorer than I'd hope.  They're not racing flats, so traction is minimal (obviously on asphalt it's always good) especially when cornering.  These don't translate into faster times for me, though I did hit my PR in a mile of 5.55 while wearing these, which I'm happy with considering I NEVER DID TRACK and was a smoker at the time!  I would recommend finding something a bit more substantial for racing (like flats, such as the ones I shall be reviewing soon!!!) and longer distance (maybeDURABILITY: I've been running in these for nearly a year, with about 200 miles on them (I switch shoes A LOT...don't judge), and they've held up nicely, they even still look fairly clean.  My boxer likes to go after my feet sometimes, and so they've withstood her constant "playful" biting and pulling (funny, she alone goes after shoes when they're ON feet), and they're still worth every mile.  I often run on the indoor track on campus (UCDAVIS!) and find poor traction on the inside of the foot, where the non-vibram material has been smooshed a bit. This can't really be avoided, just be aware if running on smooth tile or waxed surfaces! These shoes don't pick up rocks as easily as I thought they would have considering the gaps and flexy-ness on the sole, which makes for nice trips out into the wilderness, but I'd advise not going too far off road.  For that, try the NB MIMINUS TRAIL!Some have complained about water seeping in from puddles; I haven't really noticed, but whatever; wear thicker socks or suck it up.CONCLUSION: I love these shoes.  I find myself always gravitating back to them, even after buying much more expensive and elaborate types such as Nike Free Flyknit and Nike free 5.0 (which is good for me for long distance).  This shoe is nice and wide, which makes for a good feeling when the foot expands on impact, as well as providing good surface area for max contact with minimal material.  These shoes take some getting used to, no matter your running style.  Be sure to ease into them, unless you're longing for a good thrashing to the feet and calves.
summary: Always trying new shoes...good excuse to spend $

review: I'd give the watch five stars, and the band maybe two stars, or possibly three considering it's not like it fell apart, unlike some of the Timex men's watches.  It sure has faded, though, from a pretty blue to kind of grubby looking gray.  Unlike the reviewer who said her watch faded from the chlorine in the pool, I think mine has just faded from use and exposure to sweat and body oil, since it's faded on the inside of the band as well as the outside.  I rarely swim, and use my watch mostly when running.That said, I like it enough that I recently bought another one with a black band.  I am no electronic whiz-kid when it comes to watch functions, and this is the first chrono I've had that doesn't cause me to say "*^$@*!!" when trying to actually get it to do something besides just tell time!  It's very easy to use, even while in motion.  The numbers are easy to read and the alarms are audible in a noisy environment.  Overall a good functional product with a disappointing band.

review: I bought these boots a short while back and have sufficiently worn them out and about (and to work, too!) and really like them. I don't LOVE them, but I like them.The design is simple and classic--no unnecessary frou frou here, save for the little strap at the heel--and timeless. This is the kind of boot that transcends time and current trends. They fit true to size (I'm a true 6.5, but normally purchase 7 in boots to accomodate socks and/or tights). The leather is of good quality, like all my other Clarks shoes.A few notes on design, however: all my other leather boots zip up on the INSIDE (between my legs), while these zip up OUTSIDE. Trivial detail, but it took me some time to get used to. Also these are pretty form-fitting, as they have an elastic portion on the outside (covered by a leather triangle). The leather triangle sometimes manages to get folded back--nothing major but a small annoyance sometimes.All in all, these are great boots. I definitely recommend cleaning, conditioning, and sno-sealing these prior to use (and every few weeks or so). Cheers!

review: I originally bought this purse in black, white, and hot pink.  I can't tell you how many times I've grabbed the black one for casual Friday or to go run errands.  It looks GREAT with jeans, t-shirts, cardigans, and denim jackets, and I'm sure I'll use it when I dress up, too.  I imagine I'll use the white one more in the spring when I want something a little more light and fun.  I just bought the red and purple because I realized how great they would both look with what I have in my closet.The top flap closes very securely with magnetic snap button closures.  The rubber cord of the handle is sturdy and easy to loop around my shoulder.  The interior is pretty generous for such a compact size.  I easily fit an iPhone, an Umbra card case for my credit cards, plus an iPod nano, ear buds, charging cables for everything, floss, a mini tape measure, and nail file.  Down the middle of the compartment there's a thin zipper pocket that's perfect for holding cash bills.  This divider also helps keep bits organized.The only reason I didn't give this purse 5 stars is because the hardware on my black one is just a wee bit wonky in one place.  I was REALLY disappointed when I first opened the package and saw that, but I don't even notice it now.  Considering I got the black one when they were just $8, it's not that big of a deal. And while the purse might wear out quicker than a fancier, more expensive bag (and I own some of those, too), I can easily see myself getting my money's worth out these, even at $11 each, which is what I just bought the red and purple ones for.Great classic, versatile pieces.  I might come back for yellow.....

It also said on several reviews that there is a small zipper pocket inside so that you can fill it with packing peanuts or tissue paper to bulk it up and retain the shape, so, I was sold.  The hat came and, it was emerald green and looked like a leprachaun hat.  I was somewhat disappointed.  There was no zipper pocket on the inside to puff up the hat and the hat was flat.  Hallween is quickly approaching so, I decided to try to make it work, and although i'm crafty, this was alot of work.To make this a great mad hatter hat, it is possible.  I cut a two inch slit on the inside liner of the hat and filled it with puffed up tissue paper to get the shape we needed and then I sewed the slit closed again.  I removed the odd (yellow) felt "belt" around the base and the white felt "card" on the front.  Both are barely glued on and came off very easily.  I bought some black lace material, cut it into odd sized pieces, sprayed the hat with spray adhesive and started to place the layers of lace around the hat.  The spray adhesive also added a stiffness to the hat that I was looking for.  Then I used some metalic paint markers to mark the hat up and "age" it, wraped some pinkish fabric around the base and secured in the back, made a new 10/6 card and stuck it so it was coming out of the pink fabric and then I took four wooden chopsticks, spray painted them and added some beads and things to make them look like hat pins and stuck those on the other side also coming out of the pink cloth.  To top it off, I added a peacock feather towards the back and the hat is aaaamazing!  If you are planning to order this for a mad hatter costume, please be prepared that it is an awful shade of green and there is ALOT of work that needs to be done to get it to look authentic....I will say that it did ship fast and was easy to work with once I had the proper tools to transform it.
summary: Not what I expected, but made it work

Because of the good price (and you never know when you will need a magnifying glass), I will keep them. However, both of them were much "cheaper" looking than I expected. The pictures give the impression to me that they look vintage or antique, but in real life, they do not. The chain especially looks cheap. I may try to put a different chain on them in time, although I thought in the pictures that the chain with the crystals was part of the necklaces charm. Also, for a side note, the silver in the pictures looked like it had a deep red (garnet-like) stone with the blues, but it is actually a purple with blue. The copper has a garnet-like stone with amber-hued stones.Pros: Interesting magnifying glass; relatively inexpensive if you purchase during the lightning deals.Cons: Look very much like inexpensive costume jewelry, especially the chains. Does not have a vintage look to the chains, would not be mistaken as an antique.*Had I purchased them full-price, I would have sent them back from disappointment.
summary: Beware... it definitely looks like costume jewelry, not vintage jewelry!

review: I got these shoes to replace a Nike style that had been discontinued.  I run/walk about 8 miles per day on a treadmill.  The fit was fine.  I normally take a 6.5 in shoes but in athletic shoes I get a 7.  I wear running socks that are slightly bulky but these shoes accommodated that with no issues.  They felt comfortable when I put them on and I think if I just wanted a supportive comfortable shoe for everyday use or standing on my feet, they would probably be okay.  However, I have high arches and these just didn't have the arch support I need.  I have one ankle and heel that bothers me and after one day of use that foot was much worse than it normally is after the 8 miles.  I am sure it is just my particular issue not the shoe itself, but I won't be using these shoes for running again.  They are a good value for the price if you want a comfortable shoe for walking but there isn't enough support if you have any weak/vulnerable areas in your feet.  After a couple days of rest I went back to my Nikes and my ankle is fine.  So I am still looking for a Nike replacement.

review: My experience with these pants is a great testament to the idea of waiting awhile with a product until you write a review. My opinion has changed quite a bit since I first received these a few weeks ago.First of all, as many reviewers noted, they easily run one to two sizes too big, so keep that in mind when ordering. I should have trusted this advice, but because I can be really hard to fit, I bought my actual size, and then found they were much bigger than needed.I bought these in black and navy blue. Until yesterday, I'd have said that the black pants were a dream! They dress up easily, they dress down easily, and they don't look like leggings, but like actual pants. That's great. I can wear them to work confidently knowing I'm dressed appropriately. But it's a real bummer that the front button just popped off! Keep in mind that these pants are like two sizes too big for me ... I guarantee the button was under no strain. I've never even unbuttoned or unzipped the pants to take them off. I can sew something else on, so I will continue to wear them, but I wish that hadn't happened.The navy blue pants, however ... Wow. I bought those in the mistaken belief that they'd look as nice and professional as the black pants do. No. Sitting in  my office cubicle, my brother walked past me while I was wearing these, took one look, and said "Whoa! Are you wearing SWEATPANTS?" That's right, I work with my little brother, and that's right, these pants in this color do, indeed, look like sweatpants. Never mind that I had worn them with office-appropriate attire. They still looked like sweatpants, and I was wondering if my bosses were asking themselves the same question that day - "Is she wearing sweatpants?" Wow.I say select your color very carefully, and plan to wear these with tailored pieces when you need to counteract the overly casual effect that can sometimes be unintentionally achieved. Also, keep in mind that you may lose buttons or grommets, and that you probably want to buy a size or two down. Other than that, what's not to love?
summary: I want to love these, but I just can't!

review: This is the first Invicta watch I've ever owned and I am very pleased. This model is a Man's Diver's watch and is water resistant to 660 ft. or 200 meters. Even if you don't dive, you might appreciate the water resistance in case you wear it in the shower. Other than that, it looks like a nice, men's dress watch.I am a woman, but I have begun wearing men's watches because the face is larger and I can read it without glasses. The face on this watch (not including the case or bezel) is about 1 1/4" - much larger than most women's watches.The case and bracelet are stainless steel and it is hefty enough to denote quality. The face is a gorgeous, cobalt blue color. This series is available in several different face colors (some are downright garish), but this blue is very attractive. The hour markings and hands featuring a glow-in-the-dark paint so you can more easily read the time at night. There is also a date feature on the face and the crystal has a thick, magnifying area over the date. There is a marked bezel that moves in only one direction. It makes the watch look a little less dressy, but not excessively so.The bracelet looks two-toned because some of the links are highly polished and some are brushed. The clasp is a fold-over type (which allows for further bracelet size adjustment) and also has a small safety clasp. Unless you have very thick wrists, you will likely need to have some links removed to fit you properly. You can take it to a place that sells watches to have this done unless you happen to own the special tool needed. I actually took this one to Walmart and they removed the links free of charge. (Save the extra links and pins in case you gain weight or decide to gift this watch to someone else later.)The watch comes in a lovely, although wildly colored, presentation box. It is a vivid yellow, but is beautifully finished and completely padded on the interior to protect against scratches.As this watch was sent to me through the Amazon Vine program for evaluation purposes, the rubber bracelet that is part of the set was somehow not included so I cannot review that.The things I don't like are: the owner's manual/documentation is terrible! There is no mention of how to adjust the bracelet length AT ALL and the manual seems to be a generic one designed to cover most Invicta watches. If you have owned watches before that are similar to this one, you will be fine. If this is a gift for someone who has only previously owned inexpensive watches, you will absolutely need to help them learn to use it and get the band adjusted because the manual just doesn't cut it. I think this is absurd and hope Invicta will consider creating more complete and specific documentation for each model watch.The warranty is a little misleading as it is not free. You'll spend at least $50 on "shipping and handling" if you need warranty work.Still, the price is good for a watch of this quality and I think it is very attractive. If you like the looks and the features, I think you will be happy with this watch.
summary: Nice watch at a nice price

review: Skechers Shape-Ups had me at the Black Hydro XW (for Extended Wear, not extra-wide like I thought...). With knee and lower back issues AND bunions (on a size 5.5 foot), it's very difficult to find comfortable shoes that don't bind and pinch. Skechers Shape-ups did it for me! The day the Hydros were delivered, I put them on, went into the city and walked... for hours. No lower back pain, no knee pain, and NO foot fatigue, pain, pressure, or pinching! Criss-cross Z-straps with Velcro closure (albeit, you have to be into Velcro). They do give the glutes and hamstrings a workout too--secondary benefits to having a comfortable shoe I can stand or walk in for extended time.Completely motivated, I purchased 2 more pair (Hyper Blast Mary Janes, in pewter/gray and toffee/brown.) Ahhhhh! More heavenly comfort! I was quickly becoming a Shape-Ups fanatic. First time in years that I'm not compelled to kick off my shoes the first chance I get. I pretty much live in them now.So I thought it was a no-brainer to purchase the Shape-Up SRT Kinetix Response sneaker for athletic cross-training shoes. I wanted to like these so much! I was looking forward to longer, more productive workouts at the gym. Thanks to other astute reviewers, I purchased a half-size larger.When I first put them on, they were different from the other Shape-Ups. Even at 1/2 size larger, they were still binding across the toe box (although they fit lengthwise). I walked around the house with them trying to break them in (which I didn't have to do for the other Shape-Ups). This style definitely improved the side-to-side stability while keeping the marshmallow-comfy sole. But after an afternoon, my bunions were red and swollen and my big toe was numb. Alas, not a comfy shoe for the gym after all, and I returned them.If you do not have bunion issues or wide feet, I think these Shape-Ups are great cross-trainers for the gym. Their comfortable rocker sole will both prolong your workout and increase the intensity. The roll bridge and Advanced Stabilization insole provides great lateral stability and support.I remain a staunch fan of Shape-Ups, and holding out hope for the SRT Kinetix in a wider width. But the quest for comfy, supportive cross-trainers continues. (Meanwhile, it's beach season... and I'm eyeing a pair of Shape-Ups X-Wear sandals...)
summary: Great shoe that runs narrower/smaller than other Shape-ups

Grape is a good way to describe this color, it's not a true pink and has a purple hue but not exactly, it would go with a lot of pink, so not like it would clash with your pink wardrobe.  One other thing to say, like other "flip flops", a size can encompass more than one, which this does. Plus the arthritic pain has dropped off allot, giving me the ability to pick up my guitars again. Some people claim it's probably a placebo effect, but if it is a placebo effect it seems like a pretty wild coincidence that the pain has dropped off noticeably after I began wearing the bracelet.I've read a few reviews that complain about the bracelet is causing changes in skin color. The more acidic the skin is, the more this might be an issue. I wash my wrists every morning and it's not noticeable until just before bed when I wash the area again, so in my case I don't see a problem.Over all even if it doesn't work for you I think it's attractive enough to ware as a casual men's bracelet, and at a great price. So I would have to recommend it even if you only like the looks, and if it helps with pain... that's a great bonus. In my case it seems pretty obvious that the bracelet has helped with the pain in my hands, wrists and elbows so I'm a believer and highly recommend this for people suffering with similar issues.

review: This is one of those "Almost but Not Quite" purchases. They really are very pretty earrings - small and classy looking, and decent quality.  But there are three problems that are a deal breaker for me:1.  The stones in the ad photo are a deep, brilliant purple.  This is what made me decide to order them in the first place.  I am dissappointed that the stones in the pair I received are a dull, washed out, lifeless color that isn't nearly as appealing as the photo.  I just cannot get past the difference between the ad photo and the real thing.  I have seen amathysts that are not brilliant, but I didn't expect these to look like that, because the photo is so eye-popping.2.  The stones are too heavy for the size of the earring.  The metal relatively lightweight and it cannot hold it's own against the weight of the stones, so that the stones sag down to the bottom of the earring, making them even less noticable.  This, coupled with the dull stones makes them far less beautiful than the ad photo.  Another reviewer mentioned this, but I was hoping that they would sit better on my ears.  Unfortunately, I have the same problem, and don't think they are as attractive on my ears as I had hoped they would be.3.  A little bit too heavy in general.  I have VERY thin earlobes, and my piercings are almost 40 years old.  It is easy to get earrings that make my earlobes sag and look old and unattractive.  I have to wear earrings that are made extremely light so that my ears don't appear streched out and ugly.  This would probably not be a problem for most people, but for those of us who need to be careful about this, be aware that these earrings are borderline in this area.  If you have this problem, you know what I mean.They are such pretty earrings, and if only one of these things had been different, I might have kept them.  But as it is, I am sending them back and hoping for better luck next time.  The good news is that Amazon makes this kind of return VERY EASY, so if you are undecided, you might want to go ahead and order them just to see if your luck is better than mine.
summary: Pretty - But Not Quite What I Expected.....  :-(

review: This bag was a surprise.  I ordered it because I liked the bright turquoise color shown.  This was not the color I received.  It turned up as a pale robin's egg blue. It has silver hardware and the black and white zebra vinyl.  The zipper is on substantial vinyl pieces that actually make a complete closure of the top of the bag.  A snap strap can pull in the two ends to change the shape of the bag.  Each corner has an inside snap to change it from a tote to a smaller bag. Inside the lining is a bright light gold.  This lining has a vinyl feel to it.  Should be cleanable.  Another surprise was the pocket layout inside.  One zippered pocket inside on back wall, another zippered pocket in front of that pocket but still on the back wall.  (This left the center of the bag totally open rather than being a pocket divider down the middle- What a great idea!)  Two open pockets for phone, etc. on front wall.  Pockets trimmed in blue piping. Handles are attached with four silver removable keepers.  Very nice white stitching throughtout.  Outside back has another zippered pocket big enough for keys or cell phone. This bag is medium sized.  It is not large. It didn't come with the heartshaped fob.  That was a disappointment.  They should add this or remove from picture.Later Note:  I took this bag on a four day vacation and some of the white has already worn off the outside of the bag.  I am very disappointed since the bag is brand new.

review: I have in my drawer a pair of Hanes Comfort Soft underwear I bought from a local big box store about 3 years ago.  They are soft, comfortable, and breathe fairly well.  Unfortunately, these new Hanes "Comfort Soft" [NOT!!} underwear may resemble the old ones in a picture, and both are called Comfort Soft, but in real life they are simply HORRID.  The material is so rough I feel I could use them to hand scrub heavily stained pots and pans.  The problem is they are using such a rough fabric, that it is extremely irritating to my skin.  In addition, this product does NOT breath at all.  I found myself waking up with so much sweat in all the wrong places, that I had to slip off my underwear in the middle of the night and fling them across the room.  In addition, because they use such crappy material for the body, the wrap around on the waistband is just about as rough as any waist band I have ever felt.  There was no point in even wrapping these waist bands.I will NEVER buy a Hanes product again!After having tried several brands that are being sold today, I have finally found underwear that IS good.  If you want soft, comfortable underwear that breathes properly, you want Stacy Adams underwear.  It is my #1 choice.  Eddie Bauer isn't too bad either, but if I could give Stacy Adams underwear a 10 star rating, I would give Eddie Bauer underwear probably a 7 or 8 star rating, depending on the particular item selected.  And, of course, Hanes underwear deserves a ZERO star rating, in my opinion.  (Fruit of the Loom = 1 Star, just in case somebody is wondering... Merona USED to sell underwear my size, but stopped.  The Merona underwear I have left would get about a 4 or 5 star rating.  I've checked out Merona underwear at Target, and it still feels like the stuff I bought when it was available.
summary: Hanes products have really gone down hill!

review: There are at least several general types of gaits which people have. Some feet tend to over-pronate (a person who walks more heavily on the inside of their feet), over-supinate (a person who walks more heavily on the outside of their feet), or have a more-or-less "neutral" gait (not pronating or supinating excessively). The normal gait cycle involves some degree of pronation and supination. Arguably, too much pronation or supination can cause pain in your feet. Although gait issues can be hard to diagnose, it can be fairly easy for a trained doctor to tell you whether your gait falls sufficiently outside the norm to require some kind of corrective treatment.Enter Superfeet, a less costly alternative to a custom orthotic device for those in need of controlling their foot motion. As advertised, Superfeet reposition your feet inside the shoe by firmly cupping your heels, supporting your arch, and generally keeping your feet from moving in such a way as to cause pain. For those who need them, Superfeet are very effective and do their job nicely. They are also easily obtainable for a good price, and marketed as a premium replacement insole.Superfeet are very popular and the way they sell, you'd think every person has significant gait issues and requires motion control devices in their shoes. In reality this is not the case (see first paragraph). For example, I have a pretty neutral gait. I pronate just a little on one foot and actually supinate on the other a bit, but by-and-large I walk fine. However when I developed some heel pain I decided to try Superfeet because they are advertised as a solution for that. I ended up with knee pain because these forced my feet into an unnatural (for me) walking position that altered my gait and put pressure on areas that were not accustomed to it. I ended up seeing a doctor who instructed me to use cushioned insoles only instead of something rigid like this. Fortunately I was able to take advantage of the 60-day money-back guarantee for these which is a nice thing.My point is this -- Superfeet makes a great product, nothing at all against it, and if you need it I think it's a far better investment than expensive custom orthotics that do the same thing. But if you have a problem with your feet, or knees, or hips, or anything else, see a doctor before buying any devices to put in your shoes! A good orthopedist will be able to direct you regarding what sorts of inserts, if any, you will need. You may be able to figure it out on your own or by going to a running store, but if you get it wrong you may end up making matters worse. That was my story and sure it's my own fault for not starting in the right place, but it's a common mistake to make.So overall: 5 stars for the product (it does do its job) - 1.5 stars for the lack of clarity in the advertising as to the type of gait problems these are supposed to treat = 3.5 Stars.

review: At first blush, you might be tempted to dismiss this as cheap and forgettable. Note, however, the really low price, and it represents good value. I do like this watch, but you might want to buy a couple of extra. It has one small, discrete day digit. The band is kinda stiff and uncomfortable. I have only had it a couple of weeks, but it seems to keep perfect time.LOOKS NICEThe dial face, numbers, and hands are a rich, golden affair. So much so, that the contrast is pretty low and hard to see in normal light, and impossible to see in low light. The contrast from the golden dial to the black bezel and bands is rather odd, but I do like its looks, though it cannot really be called "dress".C'MON, IT IS PLASTICYes, yes: plastic, and it looks it. Also, if you wear it daily, even under light wear, I think it unlikely that it will last more than a year. That is why I suggest you buy a couple more if you really like it. At the price, you can afford to have a couple of spares. Since the battery is advertised to last 10 years, it will be ready when pull out the spares in a year or two.GLOW IN THE DARK?Well, no. The hands are luminous, so if you take it out of bright light to dark, they will glow for a few minutes before fading.

review: I'm a long-time fan of 'no-fold' wallets, but this one is just as thick as a bi-fold.Leather seems good and sturdy. I like the ID window on the outside, about 1/8-1/4" is hidden by the border, but it still displays all necessary info. (Hides the state name at the top of a CA drivers license) Cards all sit at the same depth, so your terraced pockets behind the money clip aren't staggered, they just stack on top of each other. Not bad since the front card is most used and sticks out the most. (1/2")The money functions fine, but I've never seen a wallet use such big magnets before. These magnets are big. I assumed they were earth-magnets, but they seem more like your regular fridge magnets sewed into pockets. It holds the cash just fine and opens nice and wide, but it feels as thick as a bi-fold without any cash!No, it doesn't format credit-card strips. review: Let me start by saying I returned these after wearing them for 15 minutes around the house.  I had intended to wear them to work where I'm a project manager for a construction company.  I knew once they hit the mud they'd be unreturnable.So here was my issue.  I'm a size 16, they ran up to 17, so based on previous reviews I ordered a larger size since they apparently run small.  I got them, opened them up and immediatley tried them on.  They were very tight to put on.  There is not a lot of flex, stretch or give in the Upper (I think that's what you call it).  I got them on and standing there the fit was snug, but good.  I was happy for a moment.  Then I started to walk and my heel was all over the place, lifting with every step.  I can only imagine that this would either cause the boot (or my heel) to wear quickly.  I think the problem is the leather doesn't give much to allow your foot in, so the have to leave room in the area on top of the center of your foot (once you're in) to allow your foot in and out.  Once in though, it leaves too much room for movement.  I have to say I have Muck boots and while they're not as nice looking, the neoprene makes them a lot easier to get on and off while also holding your foot nicely.  Nice try Columbia and I really appreciate you making large sizes (hence 3 stars) but you need to go back to the drawing board on this one.  I'll gladly buy/try and inproved model.

review: I will preface this review by clarifying that I am very, very hard on my shoes. I walk the dog, ride my bike, and work out in them every day. As a licensed massage therapist, I am not only on my feet all day, I am also generating a lot of force through my legs, which means I brace a lot with my feet, and in directions that most shoes are not designed for. This is also why I love rocker soles, they have saved my back from some of the wear and tear of my profession. That said, I am not impressed with the longevity on these shoes, especially for their price. After six months, the foam in the bottom of the shoe has started to disintegrate (I would include a picture if Amazon allowed this). I also think some of the support in the back of the shoe has worn down, because my heels have started to hurt in them where they previously have not. The shoes I wore before these were a similar pair of Skechers (Skechers Women's Shape Ups - Footracers Sneaker), and nothing like that happened with them. After about a year of use, the link holding the mary jane strap on them broke and turned out to be cheap metal, which is why I chose to buy MBTs this time. The prices on both pairs of shoes was comparable, and far too much to justify breaking down in a matter of months, even with heavy use. As I stated previously, I am hard on my shoes. If you are easier on yours, these may work for you. They are nicer-looking and less obvious than most rocker-soles, and I like the mary jane style as opposed to the typical athletic shoe style. However, I will not be purchasing MBTs again, as they obviously do not stand up well to my use. I will be giving New Balance a try this time around.

review: I have been on the hunt for affordable either pale blue or aqua-colored shoes for quite some time, with little luck. But I think I've finally found a winner with these! If you're looking at the aqua, it's a wonderful color: a vivid, clear aqua that, true to its nature, perfectly flits the line between blue and green. A lot of aqua-colored shoes you'll find will either tend too blue, or too green, but these are a fantastic true aqua-- a wonderful color for summer. The leather on these is buttery soft. I bought my usual size and they were a bit snug on the feet initially, but not uncomfortably so, and based on the material and my past experiences with Minnetonka shoes, I know they'll stretch. If you're between sizes, though, I might size up, or else they might be TOO snug initially. I have absolutely no fear of blisters with these, as I feel zero rubbing with them. They truly fit like a glove in that regard. The rubber outsole also seems quite durable. The reason for four stars instead of five: they are a bit wide. Not unwearably so, nor do they gape excessively, but there is definitely a small degree of gaping on the sides, and I think they'd look/fit better if they were a hair narrower. For reference, my feet are pretty standard medium width to maybe a little narrow, but I don't wear narrow shoes or anything like that-- all the shoes in my closet, like these, are B(M) width. Also, and this is either a weird quality control issue, or an equally as weird styling choice: the laces on my pair are different lengths on each shoe. Like, noticeably different. On the left shoe, the lace is very, very short, so that it doesn't even dip out past the vamp (if you'll look at the photographs here on Amazon of the shoe, the lace on the left one is contained so that it doesn't go past the stitching). On the right shoe, however the lace reaches probably a solid inch and a half past the vamp/stitching and hangs down the side of the shoe, almost down to the outsole. This is with each shoe tied with equal-sized loops, for reference. It's odd and something I can't ever recall having happen on a shoe before! I'll probably end up trimming the longer lace to match, but it's just a bit of a mind-baffling thing that they are SO different... I guess this possibly could have been a style choice, but if so, it's a strange one, because I can't imagine who would want the laces on their shoes of unequal length. As a whole, however, I really do like these shoes and would recommend them. They're a good price, seem well-made, and the leather is extremely supple.UPDATE 9/5/13:Unfortunately, these shoes just did not last. I've now had them about three and a half months, and they're simply done for. I did not wear them super frequently, either, maybe once every couple of weeks. To put the problem simply: they stretched like CRAZY. They were rather snug when I first got them, but fast forward to now and they barely even stay on my feet! The stretching didn't just occur in length, but also in width. Because of the style of the shoe (very little structure), I think the leather just stretches willy-nilly, larger and larger, until the shoe becomes basically unusable. The sides are so stretched/deformed now that they curve out (bow) in a really severe way. What was once minor gaping at the outset has turned into these shoes being so wide that, again, I can barely keep them on my feet. They were nice while they lasted-- and for the price, I did not expect them to last forever-- but I also expected them to last for more than three months. :( I can no longer recommend.

review: I purchased these shoes solely based on the concept and the very highly rated customer reviews. You must understand that I am not a professional athlete or even a fitness guru. I am trying to loose a little weight and use a treadmill for the main portion of my exercise routine.The problem I have is that whenever I walk great distances or run in typical athletic shoes, blisters develop on the back of my heel and underneath my pinky toe. I've tried various products like mole skin, band-aids and toe separators that helped a little, but never enough.I thought these shoes would make a difference, but unless your feet are perfect - perfect meaning a perfect match for these shoes, they will be uncomfortable. My big toes are just a little too wide to fit comfortably and my pinky toes are way too short - they fit maybe half way inside the toe slot. If your big toes are "chicken nugget" sized, these shoes probably aren't for you.Aside from my feet not being a perfect match for these shoes, I could not stand how far your toes spread out. It was definitely uncomfortable - and I would assume possibly even painful if you were to do some serious walking/running. Another potential issue I noticed is that these shoes are pretty rigid and I had a hard time trying to wiggle my toes unlike some people I've seen who did video reviews on YouTube.Aside from what I didn't like about the shoes, everything else was perfect. They were extremely comfortable from the ball of my foot all the way back to my heel and they seemed to be very well made.Unfortunately for me these didn't work out and I had to return them. I guess I will just stick to the toe socks and regular athletic shoes.
summary: Excellent concept but not for everyone.

review: I bought this because I was specifically looking for some nylon tanks to wear to Bikram yoga. Polyester traps in the heat whereas nylon breathes more. I've got tons of polyester tops with "wicking" capabilities but I start to roast in the middle of yoga class when I wear them. Unfortunately, polyester is everywhere is nylon is much harder to come across these days. The tag on this tank clearly indicates it is 90% polyester, 10% spandex. Another tank to add to my "do not wear to yoga" pile....Aside from the misinformation about what this tank is made out of, I'm not that impressed. I am a 36 AA and typically wear a small or medium in exercise tops. This is advertised as a compression top so I expected it to be tight but it is VERY tight and not really that stretchy. I'm not convinced it would stretch to accommodate a much larger person without riding up with every movement. The description also states it can be worn long or short but it barely hits the top of my hips when pulled all the way down so unless you have an incredibly short torso, don't expect to be able to wear this over your butt as the picture shows.Would not buy this again.

Dorfman Pacific makes fine hats.May 2013:Just got my second Dorfman Pacific Men's Twill Outback hat. My previous model was a large which was too big for me but I wore it occasionally. Now I have the medium size which fits me perfectly. I am a medium mens hat size. Amazon doesn't pack these hats without scrunching up the sides to fit into a small box in which they ship it. I bought the previous one from a hat store in San Diego mailorder and they packed it extremely well in a larger box.Anyhow, a couple of minutes carefully bending the wire rim and urging the top part to assume the desired shape yields just the hat I wanted. If you start with a perfectly flat brim, sort of like Clint Eastwoods black hat in the film. Hang 'Em High, you can modify it slightly to suit yourself. This hat has no chin strap like some outdoor hiking hats. Caution recommended on windy days. But I love it. Put your baseball hat away for summer.another hat of this general type that I own and use when the sunlight is not strong is this:Dorfman Pacific Men's 1 Piece Canvas Leather Trim Safari HatThis remains my preferred hat for summer on sunny days. When the sun is high, 99 44/100% of your face is protected by the shade it provides. In my grandfathers day, this style of hat is what lots of men wore day in and day out. Up until the 1950's when a much narrower brim became the fashion. Then, eventually, this type of hat became passé. But among older men who have already had too much sun exposure and among younger men who don't want to go there themselves, it is making a return. Dermatologist recommended.

I will say that they wash and dry still well in the washer and dryer. but I can't say how long they will actually hold up as I only bought them about a month ago. I still have a bunch of my old ones and washed them dozens of times. Yet they still come out like new.BTW - I did not buy the new ones at Amazon as the sellers here have no choice of colors. review: I think Dansko is getting closer to making a more elegant, fashionable heel while keeping their trademark comfort.  There are a few things that need to be tweaked.  I followed the other reviews and ordered one size up.  They do NOT run as big as people have said.  Unfortunately I wore them & can't return.  I then bought a 2nd pair my normal size.  yes, they are tighter around the opening, but once on, they feel fine. Not tight at all.  I think they might be a hair shorter than my other pairs, but then again I have at least 4 other pairs of dansko's and they ALL fit differently.  I do not recommend sizing up. And if you do, make sure you try them out inside before you wear them.  They do tug at the back of my socks for some reason, but still comfortable.  Lastly, the oiled leather is only pretty out of the box.  These get beat up pretty quickly.  I wish they came in more colors that would not show the scuffs as much.  Overall, definitely comfortable.

They look like their higher heeled counterparts on the top, so they finish the line from your leg or pants very nicely.  These do not look matronly in any way and that's what I was wanting.  The cut on the top will show toe clevage, so if that bothers you, keep on looking.  But for my feet, I like the low cut since it means less binding in of my feet.  I'm getting ready to start putting together my winter wardrobe and will be purchasing the black patent for this fall/winter.  I only wish they came in red!!!

review: I bought a pair of these Isotoner's at a discount store here about 6 months ago.  I immediately liked them over the other options there when I tried them on.  Unlike most other slippers that have a regular heel based sole, these Isotoner's carry an arch support that really make them feel comfortable for a good deal of time. And they also have the added benefit of a memory foam sole. If you're someone like me that lives in their slippers, takes out the trash in them, walks through wet grass to check the mail, etc, good supportive slippers can really make a difference in long term comfort.  Unfortunately with the abuse I can sometimes hand out in them they also need to be tough and durable which these slippers are not living up too.  They have stretched quite a bit since buying them, have torn at the top where I slip into them, and one has the sole already separating from the slipper. Also, the memory foam that is built in is very thin and takes the full weight of our bodies most of the time.  It's pretty much flattened out already. I may be a little tough on my slippers, but it should be noted that these babies are not cheap, and, so far they are not holding up as well as my previous slippers that I paid half the price for.  In conclusion, these Isotoner's may be very comfortable and supportive, but certainly not do they live up to their price in durability.  They stretch considerably and are already beginning to fall apart in just 6 months.  You can do better I believe.  My recommendation is to continue shopping...

review: I wanted to love this. I was super excited to get it. I was wearing a D-cup by age 12 and am now a 38G. As such, I unfortunately can't just walk into any store and buy a bra, nor can I wear cute little camisoles with thin straps - that is, without my industrial-strength bra straps showing (LOL). So, I couldn't wait to get this. Alas, it just didn't work well for me. It does not give me the comfort and support that I want.Although I got the size I normally get in Panache, this did not fit the same. The straps of the camisole dug in, twisted around and hurt after a while. The material that makes up the built-in bra is a fairly thick, foam-like material that starts at the underwire and rises up all the way to the top of the camisole. However, the top of the underwire stopped a few inches below the top of the camisole. So, that resulted in this weird lump of foam pushing forward away from my chest at the top while the underwire laid flat (I hope that makes sense). I can only assume it was designed this way to keep the top of the camisole laying flat on your skin (which it does, indeed, do), but for me this was a weird look and feeling. However, I could have lived with that if I could have had the same support as with a Panache bra, but it just wasn't so for me.

review: I bought this suit based on reviews and price. I was just starting out swimming again after not having swam laps since my teens (I am now late 30s). I signed up for the NY Triathlon and wanted a good suit that wouldn't move but also wouldn't break the bank. Speedo has a good name, so I went for it. Well, 3 months later after regular use (approx 3 swim training sessions per week) I had to buy another suit. The material on this suit just did not hold up. I am afraid that at any point now it will just disintegrate while I am wearing it, creating a very unfortunate and embarrassing wardrobe malfunction at the pool where I swim. I don't know if this is just my suit, this model, or whatever, because in the past Speedo has made a decent product, but this particular suit just did not live up to snuff. Do yourself a favor, spend a little more and buy something else. You'll wind up doing so anyway.  Also, fit wise, it seems to run a little small. review: I ordered two bags in different colors expecting them identical in every other respect. However, when I received the bags I discovered they were VERY different from each other: one was lighter, flimsier, with a side pocket lining made out of tarp-like material, which makes annoying crinkling sound, while the other was heavier, with a sturdier bottom, and more nicely finished with a nylon side pocket lining. Both bags are Bubba 7118, but the labels differ and I suspect one is an older model. I had a good look at the two bags and at the pictures of them in various colors, and I suspect that the bags with the logo on the side pocket are the newer model, while the ones with the logo above the side pocket are the older one. That would explain minor differences in straps as well as curious repetition of color varieties. While I don't care much for the older model (the tarp-like pocket noise drives me crazy), I really like the newer model. Unfortunately the older model bags are not less expensive, and it's also hard to know for sure which model you pick while shopping.Model issues aside, I'd give it four stars otherwise, because it is a nice bag, just the right size for the gym, and reasonably priced. It would be easier if the bags were marketed appropriately, but that's the downside of internet shopping.
summary: Be careful of old/new bag models sold as the same bag

review: I bought the brown oiled leather Dansko's for a vacation to the UK and returned them promptly after getting back home to the US.  I thought since I've heard great things about these shoes and since Danskos are so ugly, they have to be comfortable.  I was totally wrong!!  These shoes proved to be the absolute worst choice in footwear for European travel and for those that have low arches!The Dansko's work great for people with high arches, but I felt like I had a river rock jammed in my arch and the aftermath left me with intense pain in my arches and heels days after switching to my Crocs for the remainder of my vacation.The Danskos also have a thick, stiff arch which is a disaster when walking on uneven pavement or cobblestone as the shoe doesn't give.  I rolled my ankle countless times while walking on cobblestone and ended up with a mild sprained ankle on the first day of my vacation.  I have very strong ankles, excellent balance (I'm a yoga instructor), and I'm a lightweight so I'm not the type of person that takes a step wrong or prone to rolled ankles.Last time I vacationed in Europe, I wore $20 clog Crocs and walked 10+ miles everyday without experiencing any problems over the cobblestone.  I don't think I'll experiment with other shoes such as Danskos for the next vacation I take.
summary: The wrong shoe for low arches and European travel

review: I have big runner's thighs and decently sized calves.  It is hard to find a good pair of jeans with this body type.569s were my most affordable go-to jean for a while that had good fit and sizing, but over time, I (and my wife) found the style to be way too baggy and unbecoming.  Having a more attractive fit on the calf to ankle area, the 559s seemed like a natural progression, so I ordered a pair of these in the same size as I had for the 569's. (36x32)Using my normal size, they were way too tight on the seat, groin and thigh, albeit the waist size was fine.  I returned the pair and went up in one size (from 36x32 to 38x32) and they were a great feeling fit, but now the waist was way too loose!  Normally I could wear proper fitting jeans without a belt, but these were falling off and risking crack exposure!With a belt, and one size up, these are just right.  Not too tight or skinny to make it look like I wanted to emulate a Millenial, nor were they too baggy that I out myself as a Gen X'er stuck in the 90's.  A good go between, if flawed, if you have big thighs.
summary: Size +1 if you have big thighs, belt required.

review: This backpack has an appearance that more closely resembles a good quality traditional backpack rather than a roller back pack. The handle hides away when not in use, and extends easily with the touch of a button. The sturdy aluminum handle and high quality wheels make it easy to maneuver this steady backpack.There are plenty of pockets, including an inner padded sleeve for a laptop computer. I tried it with a sixteen inch computer with an extended battery pack, and it wouldn't fit. Most typical laptops shouldn't be a problem, though. There's an ample sized middle pocket in addition to the ones pictured, and a side pocket that could accommodate a large water bottle.Less obvious is the pouch on the back that lets you tuck the straps into it, so they don't drag on the floor. It keeps the elegant appearance when the wheels are in use.Overall, it does everything I need. If you have a larger laptop, it's more likely to fit if its outside isn't too much larger than the screen dimensions.The tag on the bag lists a limited lifetime warranty with a footnote that it's good for only five years for roller packs, so read it carefully.UPDATE: After 15 months, the handle and arm came off during normal use. The company changed its warranty procedure. Instead of sending the product to them, I had to reply to an email by attaching a photo of the damage and of the UPC tag, and answering one simple question: what happened? The rep said all I needed to say was that "it just came off." They wanted a copy of the receipt, which is easy to get from Amazon's purchase history. And they wanted to know my address. I can't see how it would be possible to process a warranty claim with any less information, so I'd say the experience was "as easy as possible." I will update this review again only if I don't get the replacement promptly without any hassle. I still consider it worthy of four stars, given that I'll get at least five years out of it, and my son will be off to college by the time the warranty ends. I probably replaced backpacks in the past every 15 months or so due to normal wear and tear, so this is still a bargain.UPDATE 2: After filling out the request, I got a reply nine days later. They asked me to let them know my top four choices for replacement, and now they want even more photos of tags, as well as a photo of the bag with a 3" x 5" hole cut in it. Perhaps they don't get the concept, but if they show me four replacements and let me pick one, then I'm making a choice. If they get to pick one for me, that means it's not my choice. Considering they only make one model of roller backpack, are they telling me that they have the right to send something completely different? That's not what the warranty says. It says they will repair or replace it. I'm not about to destroy a backpack until they assure me that they will honor the warranty, so I wrote back to them. I got a canned response saying it will take up to 12 days to get back to me.  Even if they sent out a replacement immediately, it would still be a poor way to honor the warranty given the amount of time it takes. But what do they expect my son to carry his books in in the interim until the replacement arrives? They already have a photo showing the handle broken off and know it's destroyed.Update 3: I'm lowering my rating to two stars. I got a response five days later saying I would hear back within a few business days. So far I haven't heard back. It's been 19 days from the last update, meaning 24 days since my email to them, and 36 days since my original contact. I'm not saying that's how long the process took. I'm saying that's how long the process has taken up to the time of this update and I'm still waiting to hear back.
